is going to say its name. First let's look at the i_e, which says /I/. Add the beginning /p/ /I/ and then add the /n/ /p/ /I/ /n/. Pine. When we say all of these together what word do you say? Always remember that the e on the end let's us know the i says /I/. It is important to know the difference in how short I and long I are spelled. 4.Before moving on make sure they understand by asking questions. Now we are going to listen for the /I/ in some words and whenever you hear /I/ I want you to lick your ice cream cone. (Say the following slowly) Miley likes to ride her bike in limes. Scan the classroom as you say the sentence to make sure the students understand. 5.Next we are going to get out our letterboxes ad practice spelling some words. The words will consist of 3, 4, and 5 phoneme words. Each student will have out their letterboxes and then give them the letters needed. Use the teacher set of letterboxes and letters to model for the students. I will model the first word for you. If we are using 3 letterboxes, how man phonemes is our first word going to have? Three is right! Since the e signals that the i says its name, it is silent and goes on the outside of the third box. The first word I will spell is bike. I want to ride my bike. Bike. /b/, b is going in the first box. /I/, i is going in the second box and I know the e is going on the outside of the third box. /b/ I/ /k/, so k is going to go in my third box. 6.Now we are going to spell come more words with our letterboxes. We will start off with 3 boxes. Have the students spell: tip, mike, tin, fine. 7.Excellent! Now we are going to use 4 boxes, which means our words will have 4 phonemes. Have students spell: slice, bride, trip, gripe, slime, spin. Now add one more box and we will spell words with 5 phonemes. These are a little harder but y'all are great spellers so do not worry. Have students spell: twist, strike, sprite 8.Then model to the students how to read each of the words without the letterboxes. I will show you how to read this word (point to a word that you rewrite on the board). First I see the i_e so I know the i says its name. /b/ /I/ /k/. Bike. Now it is your turn to read the words. Then have the students read the words you just previously spelled. 9.Then pass out the copies of Di and the Mice. Who likes to ride bikes? Well guess what so does Di! One day Di is riding her bike until she stops to eat for a little bit, and she sees something white in the vines! What do y'all think is in the vines? Do you think Di will be scared? What do you think Di will do? Well to find out you'll have to read with your partner. Have the students pair up and help each other read the words and use the different decoding strategies they know. 10.After they are done reading have them write a message about riding their own bike. If the students do not have a bike have them tell why they do or do not. 11.Finally for assessment have each of the students come to your desk and have them read words on the note cards you prepared earlier with pseudo words on them. This will let you see whether or not each student understands the i_e correspondence. Rebecca Neilson, "I scream for Ice cream" to the passages index. Title: How to Get Music on Animal Crossing Animal Crossing is a popular life simulation video game series developed by Nintendo that allows players to build and customize their own virtual island paradise, complete with music to set the mood. If you're looking to add some tunes to your Animal Crossing experience, this tutorial will guide you through the process of getting music on the game. Step 1: Determine Your Game Version Before proceeding, make sure you know which version of Animal Crossing you are playing as different versions have slightly different methods for obtaining music. The most recent releases include New Horizons (Nintendo Switch), Wild World (Nintendo DS), City Folk (Wii), and New Leaf (Nintendo 3DS). Step 2: Unlock the Music Player Each version has its unique way of unlocking the music player feature: * In New Horizons, once you have built the Resident Services building, go inside and talk to Tom Nook. He should give you an option to purchase a stereo system from his store. This stereo can play various songs found throughout the game. * For Wild World, after paying off your initial home loan, Tortimer will send you a letter containing a radio which plays background music when placed in your house. * City Folk also requires payment of your first mortgage before Brewster offers you a stereo at his caf . * Lastly, in New Leaf, K.K. Slider performs live concerts every Saturday night outside the town hall where he gives away CDs of his performances. Key Tip: Save up bells (the currency used in-game) to pay off loans quickly so you can access these features sooner! Step 3: Collect Songs Now comes the fun part - collecting songs! Here's how to do it depending on your game version: * New Horizons: Talk to villagers daily until they mention wanting to share a song with you. You may need to bring them gifts or fulfill requests first. Additionally, certain insects, fish, and fossils sold to Flick, CJ, or Blathers respectively can yield special medley tracks. * Wild World: Similar to New Horizons, talking to villagers regularly increases the chance of them gifting you a song. Some characters like Sable in the Able Sisters shop may sell exclusive tunes too. * City Folk: Apart from interacting with villagers, you can buy music directly from the traveling musician Rasher during his visits. * New Leaf: Visit K.K. Slider's shows every Saturday evening. After performing, he'll let you choose one song from his repertoire per week. Guideline: Patience is key here. Building relationships with your animal friends takes time but pays off eventually. Step 4: Listen to Your Collection Once you've gathered some songs, it's time to enjoy them! Place the stereos or radios in strategic locations around your home or village and switch between tracks whenever you fancy a change. Remember, sharing is caring. Feel free to share this tutorial with other Animal Crossing enthusiasts who might benefit from it. Happy gaming! First thing we do is cross over down here. Digging Up Fossils - Grades: 3-5, 6-8 Imagine finding something that lived on Earth millions of years ago. That is what paleontologists (pay-lee-en-TAH-le-jists) do. These scientists look for fossils. A fossil is what is left of an animal or plant that lived long ago. Many fossils are the bones of animals that were buried. Over many years, they got buried deeper, and the bones and nearby soil hardened into rock. Here is how paleontologists dig up fossils to study. 1. How do scientists know where to look for fossils? Sometimes weather wears away the soil and uncovers a fossil. Other times, builders find a fossil when they dig. That is a clue that there might be more fossils deep under the ground. So scientists use bulldozers to dig away chunks of rock and soil. 2. Workers then use shovels, drills, hammers, and chisels to get the fossils out of the ground. The scientists dig up the fossil and the rock around it in one big lump. They must be careful not to break the fossil as they dig. 3. Paleontologists have to keep careful records of the fossils they find. They measure, draw, and take pictures of the fossils. They use this information later, when they work with the fossils in their laboratories. 4. The parts of the fossil that stick out of the rock are sprayed or painted with a special glue. This helps to make the bones strong. Then the fossil and rock are wrapped in bandages covered with plaster. This protects the fossil so it will not break when it is sent to the lab. 5. The fossils are labeled and placed in crates with soft padding. These crates help protect the fossils from breaking. The crates are carefully loaded onto trucks and sent to the lab. There, scientists will use the fossils to learn about creatures of long ago. Think About It Do you think it is important for us to study animals that lived long ago? Why or why not? Spell Cover Write Check Teachers have plenty of tricks up their sleeves to help children learn spelling and vocabulary, with "look, say, cover, write, check" being one of the most popular. This process involves a grid of spelling words and is one of the most tried and tested methods for English-speaking children to learn how to spell words. It's an exercise that can be done at home, especially if the activity is seen as fun rather than "homework" or a competition. (Oh, and the bonus of look, say, cover, write and check is that it works just as well for adults.) Start with the game To make this spelling game fun to do at home, get your kids to assemble their own list of words they particularly like or want to practice spelling. It could be words they spot on signs as they drive home from school or words they randomly choose from the street directory or television ads. Make sure you have a lined piece of paper which can be divided into three or four columns. As the parent, it will be up to you to write neatly into the first column the chosen words to target for the spelling game. Start with: Look Look at the word which is first on the list and needs to be learned. Encourage your child to dissect it. Which bits of the spelling are easy and which are not so obvious? Get your child to circle or underline the 'hard spots', which is the difficult parts of the word, using a different coloured pen. Ask if they think the word is similar in spelling to another word that has a related meaning? Can your child break it into parts? You child should be looking at the first column on the grid of paper as they do this. Step two: Say Reciting the word out loud is a key way to remember the spelling. Ask your child if they think the spelling a reflection of the sound? If not what is different? Now the hard part: Cover Ask your child to cover the word which they have just studied and recited. Using the grid, they must now cover the list of words with a piece of paper. You can make this fun, and give them a blindfold or pirate patch to use instead of a boring old piece of paper. Now is the time to encourage your child to be "seeing" the word that is covered. Ask them questions like, "what does the word look like", "which letter combinations can you see clearly". Can your child remember the easy parts and the tricky sections. Now write the word from memory only. Keep the other sections covered and have your child write the word fresh. Now you can uncover the word and compare it with the correct spelling from the first column on the piece of paper. Check it letter by letter. If it's wrong, start again! If it's right, don't forget to lavish praise upon them and reward them. Dance Style: Rock 'n' Roll ========================= Introduction ------------ Rock 'n' roll dance is an energetic and lively partner dance that originated in the 1950s as a response to the upbeat rhythms of rock music. This type of dance incorporates various styles such as swing, jive, and boogie-woogie, resulting in dynamic movements and spins. The following steps will guide you through learning this exciting dance style. ### Things You'll Need * A practice area with enough space (preferably wood or smooth flooring) * Comfortable shoes suitable for dancing * A partner (optional but recommended) * Upbeat rock 'n' roll music Steps ----- ### Step 1: Familiarize Yourself With Basic Footwork Before diving into more complex moves, learn the basic footwork patterns for both partners. These foundational steps form the basis for most other figures in rock 'n' roll dancing. #### For Leaders (typically males): 1. Start by standing upright with your feet together, holding your partner's right hand with your left hand at shoulder level. 2. Begin with the **rock step**: step back with your left foot while shifting weight onto it, then bring the right foot next to the left without transferring weight. 3. Perform the **triple step**: take three quick steps - right foot behind left, left foot beside right, then right foot forward. 4. Repeat these two steps starting with the opposite foot (right foot instead of left). ![Basic Leader Steps]( Um like the boxer man, but it's going to be a ghost one. Checking the degree of planets in astrology is an essential skill for anyone looking to delve deeper into their birth chart or the charts of others. The degree of a planet refers to its position along the ecliptic (the Sun's apparent path) at the time of birth, measured in 360 degrees from 0 Aries. Each degree can reveal nuanced information about the expression and manifestation of that particular planet in one's life. This tutorial will guide you through the process of checking the degree of planets in astrology with clarity and practicality, ensuring that beginners and seasoned practitioners alike can benefit from this knowledge. **Step 1: Gather your tools** To begin, ensure you have access to accurate birth data including date, exact time, and location of birth. You may also need either astrological software such as Solar Fire, Win*Star, or free online resources like Astro-Seek or Astrodienst. These platforms allow users to generate a natal chart based on inputted birth details, displaying planetary positions and corresponding degrees. _Key Tip:_ Double-check all entered data before generating any chart, as even minor discrepancies can lead to significant variations in planetary placements and degrees. **Step 2: Generate your natal chart** Enter your birth data into your chosen platform and generate your natal chart. If using online resources, select 'Natal Chart,' 'Birth Chart,' or similar options after entering your details. Once generated, take note of whether the chart displays whole signs or Placidus house systems; this choice impacts where certain houses fall but does not affect planetary degrees. _Guideline:_ Familiarize yourself with basic elements of a birth chart, such as houses, aspects, and planets, if needed, prior to moving forward. **Step 3: Identify the planets** Locate each planet within your natal chart by finding its glyph representation - symbols used to denote different celestial bodies. For example, Mars appears as a circle with an arrow pointing northeastward, while Venus resembles a small hand mirror. Consult an astronomical symbol reference sheet if necessary. _Key Tip:_ Some platforms highlight planets by default; otherwise, look for circular shapes throughout the chart wheel. **Step 4: Note the planetary degrees** Once you have identified each planet, locate its respective degree(s). Most platforms list these numbers adjacent to or below each planetary glyph. Record these values for future reference. _Guideline:_ Be mindful of retrograde motion, which affects some planets' apparent backward movement along the ecliptic; these planets might appear in reverse order when listing their degrees. **Step 5: Analyze the significance of each planet's degree** Now that you possess the degrees associated with each planet, research their meanings to enhance understanding of potential influences they hold over various areas of your life. Websites such as Serennu.com offer comprehensive lists detailing specific interpretations related to individual planetary degrees. _Key Tip:_ Remember that personal interpretation plays a crucial role in deciphering astrological readings; consider multiple perspectives before forming conclusions regarding possible effects. By following this thorough tutorial, you now possess the skills required to check the degree of planets within an astrological context accurately. Armed with this newfound knowledge, further exploration becomes accessible, enabling more profound insights into both personal growth and interpersonal dynamics. I think we can be done with the ball now. Title: How to Download an Area on Google Maps on an iPhone Google Maps is a powerful tool that allows you to navigate through different locations with ease. One useful feature of Google Maps is the ability to download areas for offline usage. This can be particularly helpful when traveling to places where internet connectivity might be limited or expensive. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to do this on your iPhone. 1. **Open Google Maps:** The first step is to launch the Google Maps application on your iPhone. You can find it by swiping down on your home screen and typing "Google Maps" into the search bar, then tapping on the app icon. 2. **Search for the Location:** Once the app is open, use the search bar at the top of the screen to type in the name of the location or area you want to download. After entering the query, hit 'Search' to view the results. 3. **Zoom In/Out to Adjust Bounds:** After finding your location, zoom in or out using two fingers (like pinching) until you have selected the exact area you wish to save for offline access. Remember that larger areas will require more storage space on your device. 4. **Tap the Name of the Place:** At the bottom of the screen, there should be a label showing the name of the place. Tap on this label. 5. **Select 'Download' Option:** A new menu will appear after tapping the place name. Scroll down until you see the 'Download' option in grey. Tap on it. 6. **Name Your Offline Map:** A pop-up window will appear asking you to name your offline map. Make sure it's something descriptive so you know what area it covers later. Then tap 'Save'. 7. **Wait for Download Completion:** Depending upon the size of the mapped region and your internet speed, the download process may take several minutes. During this time, ensure your phone remains connected to WiFi as downloading over cellular data could lead to high data charges. 8. **Check Downloaded Areas:** To check all downloaded maps, go back to the main page of Google Maps, click on the hamburger menu (three horizontal lines) located at the top left corner, select 'Offline Maps', and voila! All your saved regions are listed here. Remember, these offline maps expire after 15 days if you don't update them, but they're easy to refresh; just follow steps 4-6 again. Also note that while offline mode provides navigation functionality, features like traffic information won't be available since they rely on real-time data which isn't accessible without an active internet connection. By following these simple yet thorough instructions, you'll never get lost without WiFi or worry about hefty roaming fees ruining your travel budget. Happy navigating! [Linda comes back in the room with a small glass of apple juice which she hands to Nina.] [points to a toy plastic baby bottle containing a white liquid which looks like milk] Title: How to Change Your Username on OurWorld.com OurWorld is a popular online gaming platform that allows users to create their own unique avatars and explore a virtual world filled with games, social interactions, and customization options. One way to personalize your experience on OurWorld is by choosing a username that reflects your personality or interests. If you're new to the platform or have decided that your current username no longer represents you, this tutorial will guide you through changing your username on OurWorld.com. **Step 1: Log into Your Account** To begin the process of changing your username, first log into your account on the OurWorld website ([www.ourworld.com](http://www.ourworld.com)). Enter your email address and password in the designated fields at the top right corner of the homepage. Once logged in, you will be directed to your personal profile page. Key tip: Make sure you remember your login credentials, as you will need them to access your account and make changes. **Step 2: Access Your Account Settings** From your profile page, locate and click on the gear icon at the top right corner, which represents the settings menu. A dropdown list will appear; select 'Account Settings' from the available options. This action will take you to the main account management section where you can modify various aspects of your account, including your username. Guideline: Keeping your account information up-to-date ensures a smooth user experience and better security. **Step 3: Navigate to the Personal Information Tab** Once inside the Account Settings area, look for tabs along the left sidebar. Click on the tab labeled 'Personal Information.' Here, you will find several editable fields containing details about your account, such as your display name, date of birth, gender, country, and email address. Key tip: While you are updating your username, consider reviewing other personal information to ensure its accuracy. **Step 4: Edit Your Display Name** Locate the field labeled 'Display Name,' which shows your current username. To change it, simply delete the existing text and replace it with your preferred username. Note that there may be character limits and restrictions regarding special characters or spaces, so choose wisely. Guideline: Select a username that adheres to community standards and does not contain offensive language or personal identifiers. **Step 5: Save Changes** After entering your new username, scroll down to the bottom of the page and click on the blue button labeled 'Save Changes.' By doing so, you confirm that you accept any terms related to modifying your username, and you commit to abiding by the rules set forth by OurWorld. Key tip: Double-check your spelling before saving, as once changed, your old username cannot be recovered. **Step 6: Confirm Successful Update** Upon clicking 'Save Changes', you will be redirected back to your profile page. At the top, next to your avatar, verify if your username has been updated successfully. You should now see your newly chosen username displayed prominently. Guideline: Remember that some areas within OurWorld might still show your old username temporarily until all systems update accordingly. Congratulations! With these six simple steps, you have learned how to change your username on OurWorld.com. Enjoy exploring the virtual world with your refreshed identity! What color is this one [ball magnet]? Title: How to Clicker Train a Bird Introduction: Clicker training is a popular and effective method used to train birds and other animals. It relies on positive reinforcement, which means rewarding the bird when it performs the desired behavior. This type of training not only strengthens the bond between you and your pet but also makes learning fun and enjoyable for both parties. Here's a comprehensive guide on clicker training your bird, step-by-step. Step 1: Gather Necessary Materials * Clicker - A small plastic device that produces a distinct clicking sound when pressed. They are available at most pet stores or online. * Target stick - A short dowel with a blunt end, often decorated with a bright color or a ball, serving as a focal point for your bird during training sessions. * Treats - Choose healthy, bite-sized treats that can be given frequently without filling up your bird too quickly. Examples include nuts, seeds, fruits, or vegetables. Step 2: Charge the Clicker "But why should you forget Him afterwards? Why not make Him your Friend, as He desires to be?" Title: How to Check Your Windows Search Version in Windows Vista Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through checking your Windows Search version in Windows Vista. By following these steps, you can ensure that your system is up-to-date with the latest search functionality provided by Microsoft. This process may also help troubleshoot any issues related to searching files or folders within your operating system. Step 1: Access Control Panel Click on the Start button located at the bottom left corner of your screen. From the Start Menu, click on the Control Panel option. This action opens the Control Panel window where various settings are managed. Key Tip: If you find the default view confusing due to its category arrangement, change it to 'Small Icons' or 'Large Icons' from the upper right corner View menu for easier navigation. Step 2: Open Programs and Features In the Control Panel window, locate and select the Programs and Features icon. It might be under the subheading 'Programs'. Clicking this icon reveals all installed programs and features on your computer. Guideline: Be patient as the list populates; depending upon how many applications are installed, this could take several seconds. Step 3: Sort Installed Updates Alphabetically (Optional) By clicking once on the 'Name' column header, you sort the entire list alphabetically. Scroll down until you reach the section starting with 'Windows Search'. Skipping this optional step leaves the entries listed based on their installation date which isn't ideal when looking for specific software like Windows Search. Step 4: Identify Windows Search Version Look for an entry titled similar to 'Windows Search XX.X.XXX.XXXX', where X represents digits indicating the exact version number. The most recent versions usually appear closer to the top. Remember the version number displayed here since it indicates the currently installed version of Windows Search. Key Information: Different builds have different bug fixes and improvements, so knowing your current build can assist in identifying whether certain problems are already addressed or if they require updating. Step 5: Verify Whether You Have Latest Version Visit Microsoft Support Site to check the latest available version of Windows Search for Vista. Compare the version number obtained earlier against what Microsoft lists as the newest release. Conclusion: Now that you know how to check your Windows Search version in Windows Vista, maintaining an updated system becomes simpler. Regularly performing these checks ensures optimal performance and security while using your PC. And, uh, I took him to the doctor last week. Ask Cromer if he can do it. Title: Check How Old Your Account Is on Roblox Roblox is a popular online gaming platform that allows users to create their own games or play games created by others. If you're wondering how old your account is on Roblox, this tutorial will guide you through the process step-by-step. Step 1: Log in to Your Roblox Account Visit the Roblox website (www.roblox.com) and click on the "Log in" button at the top right corner of the page. Enter your username or email address and password to log in to your account. This step is necessary because only registered users can access their account information, including the creation date. Key tip: Make sure you remember your login credentials correctly; otherwise, you won't be able to access your account details. Step 2: Access Your Account Settings Once logged in, hover over the gear icon located at the upper right corner of the screen, then select "Settings" from the dropdown menu. This action takes you to your account settings where you can view and modify various aspects related to your profile. Guideline: The gear icon represents the 'settings' option across most websites and applications today. It has become an industry standard symbol recognized globally. Step 3: Navigate to the Account Info Tab After clicking on 'Settings', look for the tabs along the left side of the new window. Click on the second tab labeled "Account Info". Here, you will find crucial data about your account, such as your username, email address, and join date - which indicates when your account was first created. Did You Know? Join Date also signifies your membership tenure within the community, making it a valuable piece of personal history with the platform! Step 4: Locate Your Join Date Scroll down until you see the section titled "Personal Information." Within this area, locate the line item named "Join Date." This field displays the exact date when your account was initially registered on Roblox. Helpful Hint: Dates are usually displayed in the format Month Day, Year (e.g., January 15, 2010). Remember that daylight saving time changes might affect timestamps depending on regional laws and practices. Step 5: Calculate Your Age Based on Your Join Date To determine how old your account is, simply subtract the year part of your join date from the current year. For example, if your join date reads December 26, 2008, your account would currently be around 13 years old (assuming we're calculating during late 2021). Important Note: Keep in mind that seasons and months pass differently throughout a calendar year, so while your account may have turned a certain age digitally speaking, its actual anniversary could fall before or after the numerical milestone based on real-world timing. By following these steps carefully, you now know how to check the age of your Roblox account accurately. Happy Gaming! In the book it told that um... Title: How to Dress Your Doll on Stardoll Stardoll is a popular online fashion game where you can dress up virtual dolls with various clothing and accessories. The goal is to create stylish outfits that reflect your personal taste or match certain themes. This tutorial will provide an in-depth guide on dressing your doll on Stardoll while offering helpful tips and tricks along the way. **Step 1: Logging into Stardoll** First, visit <https://www.stardoll.com/> and log in using your account details. If you don't have an account yet, sign up for one as it's required to access the site and play the game. **Step 2: Accessing the Closet** Once logged in, click on the "Closet" button located at the bottom of the screen. This action takes you to your virtual closet, which stores all the clothes and accessories available for your doll. **Step 3: Choosing Clothes and Accessories** Browse through different categories such as Tops, Bottoms, Shoes, Outerwear, and Accessories by clicking on them. Select items you wish to use by clicking on their respective thumbnails. Selected items will be added to your 'Starbag'. Key tip here is to choose pieces that complement each other both in terms of color scheme and style. **Step 4: Equipping Items onto Your Doll** After adding desirable items to your Starbag, return to the main page (Home) and select your chosen doll from the 'My Dolls' section. Now click on the "Change Look" option. Here, drag and drop selected items from your Starbag onto appropriate areas on your doll - tops go over the torso, pants/skirts under the top but above shoes, etc. Remember, order matters! **Step 5: Customizing Further** To add more flair to your outfit, consider using hats, glasses, jewelry, bags, etc., found under the Accessories category. These elements can truly make an ensemble stand out. However, ensure they do not overwhelm the overall look; balance is crucial. **Step 6: Saving and Sharing Your Creation** When satisfied with your creation, save it by clicking on the disk icon next to the 'Change Look' button. You may also share your design via social media platforms directly linked within Stardoll. Always remember to review and finalize before saving or sharing. **Additional Tips:** * Experimentation leads to improvement. Don't hesitate to try new combinations and styles. * Keep track of trends inside and outside Stardoll to stay current and inspired. * Participate in community challenges and contests to enhance creativity and skillset. * Utilize the 'Favorites' feature wisely. Mark frequently used items for quick access later. * Be mindful of the occasion or theme when selecting attire. A casual day calls for different garb than a formal event. By following this comprehensive tutorial, mastering the art of dressing your doll on Stardoll becomes much easier. Um well the book seemed to make her upset for some reason. Rationale: In order to learn to read and spell words, children need to understand digraphs so they can match letters to their phonemes. In this lesson, children will learn to recognize the ou = /ow/ in both spoken and written words They will learn to do this by practicing reading and spelling words containing ou = /ow/. I will use a letterbox lesson to help with instruction of this correspondence. Materials: Primary paper, pencil. The Napping House by Audrey Wood. Elkonin letter boxes. letter: s.h,o.u.t.l,d,m.n,f,a,and b, flashcards of the letterbox words, and a chart with the following tongue twister on it: I found out about the loud shout. Procedures: 1. Introduce the lesson by asking the children if they have ever gotten hurt and said "ow!" I will then tell them that when the two letters o and u get together, they make that sound. I will write these letters on the hoard. Today we are going to work on words that have the ow sound in them and are spelt with an on. 2. I want all of you to repeat this tongue twister after me. "I found out about the loud shout" Good! Did you hear the ow sound in those words. Now I want you to hold out the ow sound when we say the tongue twister. I fou------nd ou-------t abou------t the lou-------d shou--------t. Excellent job! 3. Next. I will have the children take out their letterboxes and the letters listed ahove. Now we are going to spell the words that I've just said. Remember the ow sound is made when the o and u get together. I will model one example on the hoard for the students and then we will go through words one at a time as the students spell the words in their letterboxes. Because the o and u together make one sound, they should put them in one box. Words to spell: out = ou/t loud = l/ou/d shout = sh/ou/t found = f/ou/n/d mound = m/ou/n/d 5. We will then practice this sound by reading the hook The Napping House. I will read The book to the students and they will say ow! when they hear the ou sound. The students will then read the book themselves and write down the words that have the ou correspondence in them. 6. For review, I will hold up flash cards of the words we have learned with the ou sound and the children will be called upon one at a time to say what is on the card. 7. For assessment. I will pass out a worksheet in which the students will have to circle the words that have the ou = /ow/ correspondence in them. They will fill it out and turn it in. References: Murray, Bruce and Lesniak T.(1999) The Letterbox Lesson: A Hands-On Approach to teaching decoding. Wood. Audrey. Dick's feelings on actually reaching the top in spite of his doubts were supplemented by a mortal fear that the fiddling might even stop at this supreme moment; which prompted him to convey a stealthy whisper to the far-gone musicians, to the effect that they were not to leave off till he and his partner had reached the bottom of the dance once more, which remark was replied to by the nearest of those convulsed and quivering men by a private nod to the anxious young man between two semiquavers of the tune, and a simultaneous "All right, ay, ay," without opening the eyes. Fancy was now held so closely that Dick and she were practically one person. Have you ever watched your child put together a puzzle? A lot of thought goes into figuring out which piece goes where. First she has to consider the shape and size of each piece to see if it will fit into a particular space. If the shape looks right, then she has to figure out which way it goes in. She might have to rotate it or flip it so it aligns properly. As your child does the puzzle, she is using spatial skills. Manipulating 3-D objects such as pieces of pasta is another way to practice these Helping your child recognize shapes and how they relate to one another by flipping and rotating them is key to developing the spatial reasoning skills necessary for doing geometry later on. Your child uses these skills everyday-for example, when she determines if a shoe goes on the left or right foot and then positions it so it goes on correctly. Geometry and spatial skills 3-6 year olds Follow the Pasta Path Take It Further Open up a box of rigatoni pasta. Talk with your child about the shapes she sees in the pasta. Notice how some pieces are straight, while other pieces curve to the right or to the left. Show your child how she can make a path with the pasta pieces by lining them up piece-by-piece. She can change the direction of the path by choosing pieces that curve left or right. How to Do It: - Oh dear! The Cat has become separated from his friends Thing One and Thing Two. Ask your child to help them get back together. - Print out the picture of the Cat separated from Thing One and Thing Two. Use a marker or crayon to draw a wavy line that connects them. The wavy line should be done in a way that your child can easily follow it with pieces of pasta. - Give your child a handful of dried rigatoni pasta and ask her to line the pieces up end-to-end so that they follow the curvy path you drew. Before she puts down each piece, tell her to look carefully at the path and decide if she needs a straight piece, a piece that curves right, or a piece that curves left. - When she finishes building the pasta path, praise her and say, "Great job! Now the Cat can get back together with Thing One and Thing pasta face. Create a circle out of the pasta pieces, and then use other pieces for the eyes, nose, ears, and mouth. Ask your child if her pasta person is happy or sad. Go, Do. Go! by P. D. For a decimal, move the decimal point two places to the left or divide by 100. 94.6% = 0.946 Reading the decimal in words we get -- nine hundred forty-six thousandths or Remember that "percent" means "per hundred". So 94.6% means 94.6 per hundred, which is the same as Either use a calculator or move the decimal point 2 places to the left in the numerator. Either way, you get 0.946. Reading the decimal, we get 946 thousandths. Or We have to reduce that. Both the numerator and the denominator are divisible by 2. Dividing both by 2 we get Now the problem is trying to figure out whether or not 473 has any factors at all, let alone any in common with 500. Using the rules of divisibility, we can see that 473 is not divisible by 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, or 10. Trying other numbers shows that 473 is not divisible by 7, but it is divisible by 11: 473 / 11 = 43. However, the only factors of 473 (11 and 43) are both prime, and neither goes into 500, so 473/500 is reduced. Math - Writing to Explain Draw a decimal model and write 0.78 as a fraction in ... The anatomy of a squid is quite interesting, so if you haven't given it much thought you may want to look closer at them. The first thing you will notice is that they are symmetric along the length of their bodies. This allows them to easily move along in the water. The head of a squid is different than anything else you have seen that lives in the water. It is much larger than the body. It is flat but it has a large volume to it. The size and shape of it is going to vary depending on the species of squid you are referring to. A squid features eight arms that have suckers along the length of it. They also feature only two tentacles. Many people assume the arms are on the wrong end of the squid. This is because they assume the fin at the end of it is really the head. However, the head is located close to the arms and the tentacles. The mantle is the cavity of the body for a squid. This helps to protect them as they move in the water. It also serves as a place for them to maintain the nutrients they take in for their own survival. Plenty of research has been done on the topic of anatomy relating to the digestive system. It is extremely complex and that is always more interesting to researchers. The food is taken to the stomach region and then taken to what is known as the caecum. This is where the digestion process ends up taking place. From there it moves to the liver and is absorbed by the body. It is quite interesting to discover that the squid has three hearts. There is the central heart which functions normally to circulate the blood through the body. The other two work to supply blood to the gills. The mouth of a squid is very small and it is beak like. You may have to look closely on one in order to locate it. They also have two small eyes that they use to see what is going on around them. The squid also features a very large sized brain compared to its overall body size. They have the ability to use a variety of senses. For example squid are extremely sensitive to touch. They also have receptors on their body that make them very sensitive to taste as well. It is believed they have a taste ability that is about 1,000 times better than most humans. While squid are color blind, they do have excellent eyesight. They are able to see both in the shallow water and deeper regions. They can see well in the day as well as during the night time. Squid have the ability to see in a range of 360 degrees too which is quite interesting. They call also smell quite well. This is accomplished by using pits and glands that are found underneath their eyes. The overall anatomy of the squid is different from other types of creatures found in the water. However, as you can see it does work out quite well for them. Their survival in the water depends on their unique anatomy. They have continued to evolve too over the course of time in order to continue being a part of our world. Studying the anatomy of the squid allows researchers to find out more about that evolution process. It also helps them to understand the survival of these creatures. Since they offer a source of food for many other animals out there it is important for us to readily identify what makes them move and function as they do. = = = children/CHIDES_NA/Brown/Sarah/030313.cha = = = I haft ago see XXX the food stamp people. It applies to all physical phenomena, so long as gravitation is not significant. Title: How to Deal with a Friend Who Steals Your Men Introduction: It can be incredibly disheartening when a friend starts pursuing someone you are interested in or have already been involved with. This situation is complex and requires careful handling to preserve your relationships and self-esteem. Here's a comprehensive guide on dealing with such a scenario while maintaining your dignity and friendship. Step 1: Acknowledge Your Feelings Before taking any action, acknowledge how you feel about the situation. Are you hurt? Angry? Confused? Understanding your emotions will help you respond appropriately rather than react impulsively. Give yourself time to process these feelings before proceeding. Key Tip: Remember that it's okay to experience negative emotions; they're part of being human. However, don't let them cloud your judgment or dictate your actions. Step 2: Evaluate the Situation Objectively Try to view the situation from an outsider's perspective. Is there a misunderstanding? Have boundaries been crossed unintentionally? Or has your friend deliberately acted against your interests? An objective assessment can prevent unnecessary drama and ensure fairness in your response. Guideline: Avoid jumping to conclusions. Instead, gather all relevant information before making decisions. Step 3: Communicate Openly Express your concerns honestly but tactfully to your friend. Use "I" statements instead of blaming language ("I felt upset when..." versus "You always do this"). Be clear about what behaviors bother you without accusing or attacking your friend. Key Tip: Choose the right place and time for this conversation - somewhere private where both of you feel comfortable speaking openly. Step 4: Listen Actively Allow your friend to share their side of the story. They might not realize the impact of their actions or may have valid reasons for behaving as they did. Active listening involves paying full attention, showing empathy, and refraining from interrupting until they finish speaking. Guideline: Nonverbal cues like nodding and maintaining eye contact show that you're engaged in the conversation. Step 5: Seek Mutual Understanding Reach a mutual understanding based on open communication. If your friend acknowledges your concern and promises to respect your feelings moving forward, give them another chance. Trust is built over time, so patience is essential here. Key Tip: Forgiveness doesn't mean forgetting. It means choosing to move past the incident despite knowing it happened. Step 6: Set Boundaries If your friend continues to pursue the person you're interested in after discussing the issue, set firm boundaries. Let them know what behavior crosses the line and stick to your guns if those lines get breached again. Guideline: Make sure your boundary isn't punitive or controlling. It should protect your emotional wellbeing without restricting your friend's freedom unfairly. Step 7: Reevaluate the Friendship Consider whether this pattern of behavior aligns with the kind of friendship you want. If your friend consistently undermines your romantic prospects, ask yourself whether they truly value your happiness. Key Tip: Prioritize friends who uplift and support you rather than compete with you. Conclusion: Navigating situations where a friend steals your men is challenging yet manageable. By following these steps, you can maintain your self-respect, nurture healthy relationships, and grow stronger through adversity. You see the pin the tail on the donkey? The one thing which seems to remain the same in almost all versions of the stories, ancient and modern, is the background of mythology, that great, gloomy cycle of gods, with the ever-recurring note of Fate which seems to have impressed all searchers in myths alike, and which inspired Wagner when he formed his mystical, solemn Fate motif. [cup falls on floor, Mat looks for it] Title: How to Get a Hot Girlfriend ================================= Getting a hot girlfriend can be an exciting yet challenging experience. It requires confidence, patience, and genuine effort to understand what she wants in a relationship. This article will guide you through the process with clear instructions, helpful tips, and useful guidelines. ### Understanding Yourself Before starting your journey towards getting a hot girlfriend, take time to understand yourself better. Knowing who you are and what you want from a relationship is crucial. Here's how: 1. **Identify Your Values**: What matters most to you? Honesty, loyalty, kindness, ambition? Make sure these align with her values too. *Why it helps*: Having shared values creates a solid foundation for any relationship. 2. **Self-Assessment**: Recognize your strengths and weaknesses. Work on improving areas that need attention but also embrace your uniqueness. *Why it helps*: Confidence attracts; being comfortable in your skin makes you more appealing. 3. **Set Realistic Expectations**: Don't aim solely for physical attraction. Focus on finding someone compatible emotionally, intellectually, and socially as well. *Why it helps*: A balanced approach increases chances of a successful, lasting relationship. ### Preparation Stage Once you have a good understanding of yourself, prepare for the dating scene. 4. **Improve Appearance**: Dress nicely, groom properly, exercise regularly. Not only does this boost self-confidence, but it also shows respect for yourself and others. *Key Tip*: Choose clothes that suit your body type and personal style. 5. **Expand Social Circles**: Join clubs, attend social events, volunteer - anything that exposes you to new people and experiences. *Guideline*: The broader your network, the higher the probability of meeting 'the one'. 6. **Develop Good Communication Skills**: Practice active listening and clear expression of thoughts. These skills are vital in building strong connections. *Key Tip*: Show genuine interest in what she says by asking questions and responding thoughtfully. ### Meeting Potential Partners Now that you're ready, start meeting potential partners. Remember, rejection is part of the game so don't get discouraged if things don't work out immediately. 7. **Initiate Conversations**: Start conversations naturally without sounding rehearsed or insincere. Talk about mutual interests, current happenings, or even jokes. *Key Tip*: Maintain eye contact and smile often during conversation. 8. **Show Interest**: Ask open-ended questions to learn more about her. Listen attentively and respond appropriately. *Why it helps*: Shows you value her opinions and perspectives, making her feel special. 9. **Be Respectful**: Treat all women with kindness and dignity, regardless of their appearance or behavior. *Guideline*: Respect is attractive and sets the tone for healthy relationships. ### Building a Connection After initial interactions, focus on building a deeper connection based on trust and friendship. 10. **Spend Quality Time Together**: Engage in activities both enjoy doing together. Shared hobbies strengthen bonds. *Key Tip*: Balance between group dates (with friends) and private ones (just the two of you). 1 Do you think one of the guys should come drink some? [pointing to a man climbing a ladder to gather apples] Title: How to Change the Default Font in Microsoft Word Microsoft Word is a powerful word processing tool that offers numerous customization options, one of which is changing the default font. This feature can be particularly useful when you want all new documents to have a specific look or if you're working on a project where consistency in typography is crucial. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to change the default font in Word. **Step 1: Open a New Document** Start by launching Microsoft Word and creating a new blank document. It might seem counterintuitive to start with a new document instead of changing settings in an existing file, but this method ensures that your changes will apply to every future document. **Step 2: Access the Font Dialog Box** Next, we need to access the 'Font' dialog box. You can do this either through the 'Home' tab on the Ribbon Interface or via the keyboard shortcut `Ctrl + D` (or `Cmd + D` on Mac). The former method involves clicking on the 'Font' group within the Home tab, then selecting 'Font' from the dropdown menu; the latter simply opens the same dialog box directly. **Step 3: Select Your Desired Font** In the opened 'Font' dialog box, under the 'Font' section, you'll see a list of available font styles. Choose the font face you wish to set as the new default. Key tip here: Make sure it's a font installed on your system so Word can recognize it. **Step 4: Set the Font Size** Just below the font faces, there's another dropdown labeled 'Size'. Click on it and select the size you prefer for your default font. Remember, different projects may require different sizes, so choose based on typical usage or overall design requirements. **Step 5: Customize Other Options (Optional)** Depending on your needs, you could also adjust other parameters like font color, effects (like bold, italic, underline), etc. These are optional steps and won't necessarily affect readability unless used excessively. **Step 6: Apply to All Documents Based on Normal Template** Once you've made your selections, click on the 'Default...' button at the bottom left corner of the dialog box. A confirmation popup window titled "Template" should appear, warning you that any changes made here will affect all documents based on the Normal template. Check the checkbox next to "Yes, I want to update all normal templates now," then hit OK. By choosing to update all normal templates, you ensure that these settings become the standard across all new documents. If you only wanted them applied to the current document, you would skip this step. **Step 7: Confirm Changes** A final message box will confirm that the new font has been successfully saved as the default. From now on, whenever you create a new document, it will automatically use the chosen font style and size. Key Guidelines: - Always remember to save your work before making significant changes like setting defaults. - Be aware that altering defaults applies globally, affecting every new document created henceforth until changed again. - Consider legibility and professional standards when deciding upon font type and size. With these simple yet effective steps, you now know how to change the default font in Microsoft Word, enhancing both personal preference and professional cohesion in your typed content. Title: How to Delete a Post on OfferUp on iPhone or iPad OfferUp is a popular online marketplace where users can buy and sell items within their community. If you have listed an item for sale on OfferUp using your iPhone or iPad and wish to remove it, follow these steps to delete the post: 1. Open the OfferUp app on your iPhone or iPad. The app's icon looks like two interlocked capital 'O' letters in blue and white against an orange background. Tap on this icon to launch the application. 2. Sign in to your account if necessary. Enter your email address or phone number along with your password to access your OfferUp profile. This step ensures that you are able to manage your listings effectively. 3. Navigate to your Seller Profile. To do so, tap on the person-shaped icon located at the bottom right corner of the screen. This will take you to your seller profile page. 4. View Your Active Listings. Here, you will see all the active listings associated with your account. You may need to scroll down depending upon the number of posts you currently have live. Familiarize yourself with the layout so you can locate specific listings quickly when needed. 5. Identify the Post You Want to Remove. Locate the post you want to delete among your active listings. Once found, prepare to initiate the deletion process. 6. Initiate Deletion Process. Swipe left over the listing thumbnail until you see three options appear - 'Edit', 'Deactivate', and 'Delete'. Note that these might vary based on the version of the app or iOS being used. 7. Select 'Delete'. Tapping on 'Delete' starts the irreversible removal process of the selected post from OfferUp. Key Tip: Make sure you really want to permanently delete the post as there is no confirmation message after selecting 'Delete'. 8. Confirm Action. After tapping 'Delete', you'll be asked to confirm whether you truly intend to delete the post. Choose 'Yes, Delete' to finalize the action. A pop-up notification should then inform you that the post has been successfully deleted. 9. Check Your Active Listings Again. Go back to your seller profile page and check your active listings again to ensure that the deleted post is indeed gone. It shouldn't show up anymore once properly removed. 10. Exit the App. When satisfied that everything is in order, press the home button (if available) or swipe upwards from the bottom edge of the display (on newer iPhones without physical buttons) to close the OfferUp app. This concludes the tutorial. Remember, deleting a post cannot be undone. Always double-check before proceeding with the deletion to avoid any inconvenience. Also, note that while we focused on iPhone and iPad devices here, similar procedures generally apply across different platforms supported by OfferUp. You can put it under his jacket. And he was looking at the clock. Propaganda was carried much further by the "Sacra Congregatio de Propaganda Fide" of the Catholic Church. This committee, founded in 1622 by Pope Gregory XV, had action branches in most European countries. These were the local branches of the Inquisition, which sought out heretics. With torture and the threat of death by burning at the stake, they forced heretics to recant (to publicly withdraw their previous beliefs). The objective was to remove all challenges to the supremacy of the Church in matters of belief. The 1578 handbook for inquisitors noted "Punishment does not take place primarily and for the correction and good of the person punished, but for the public good in order that others may become terrified and weaned away from the evils they would commit". Controlling your wife's spending can be a challenging task, but with clear communication, mutual understanding, and careful planning, you can work together to create healthier financial habits. Here is an in-depth guide to help you get started: **Step 1: Discuss Your Concerns** Openly communicate your concerns about her spending patterns without being judgmental or accusatory. Explain that you want to collaborate on achieving better financial stability as a couple. This conversation should ideally take place at a calm time when both parties are receptive to discussing finances. *Key Tip*: Make sure this discussion focuses on shared goals rather than individual faults. It's essential to approach the topic from a partnership perspective instead of placing blame. **Step 2: Establish Shared Financial Goals** Identify what you both hope to achieve financially - whether paying off debt, saving for retirement, purchasing a home, etc. Having common objectives will encourage teamwork and compromise regarding spending decisions. *Guideline*: Be realistic about these targets, setting short-, mid-, and long-term benchmarks. Review them regularly to ensure they remain relevant and attainable. **Step 3: Evaluate Current Spending Habits** Track all expenses (yours and hers) over several months using apps, spreadsheets, or pen and paper. Categorize every expenditure into necessities, discretionary items, bills, savings contributions, etc., then analyze where most money goes and identify potential areas for reduction. *Tip*: Consider involving a third party like a financial advisor if discussions become heated or unproductive. **Step 4: Create a Realistic Budget Together** Based on your findings, develop a budget reflecting your income, necessary costs, and agreed-upon limits for variable spending categories such as entertainment, dining out, personal care, hobbies, etc. Remember to allocate funds towards your collective financial objectives too. *Guideline*: Aim for a balance between restrictiveness and flexibility; allow some room for spontaneity while maintaining control over overall spending trends. **Step 5: Implement Tools to Monitor Spending** Utilize budgeting tools (apps, software, envelopes filled with cash), linked bank accounts, or joint credit cards to keep track of daily expenditures against the established budget. Regularly review progress together. *Key Tip*: Transparency is crucial here. Both partners must have access to all financial information and updates to foster trust and accountability. **Step 6: Schedule Periodic Check-ins** Set regular meetings (weekly, bi-weekly, monthly) to discuss ongoing adherence to the budget, celebrate successes, address challenges, adjust limits if needed, and reinforce positive behaviors. These conversations also serve as opportunities to strengthen your relationship by working through difficulties cooperatively. *Guideline*: Maintaining open lines of communication ensures continued alignment around financial priorities and fosters continuous improvement in managing household expenses. **Step 7: Encourage Personal Responsibility** While monitoring communal spending, empower your wife to manage her own discretionary budget category independently. By giving her autonomy within certain boundaries, she learns self-control and decision-making skills related to spending choices. *Tip*: Offer support and encouragement along the way, acknowledging efforts made towards sticking to the plan. Remember, controlling your wife's spending isn't solely about limiting expenditures; it involves building a solid foundation for open dialogue, collaboration, and responsible money management practices as a united front. The Ford Mustang is a very popular American sports car built by the Ford Motor Company beginning in 1964. The first cars were built in Ford's Dearborn, Michigan factory on March 9 of that year and the car was first shown to the public on April 17 at the New York World's Fair. It did not cost a lot of money, but it was still very fancy and looked like a much more expensive car. People could order their Mustang with many options and body styles, such as a convertible, 2+2 (fastback), or coupe. Many different engines were available, from the 170 CID six-cylinder to some of Ford's biggest and most powerful V-8 engines in later years. This meant car buyers could have a Mustang that saved gas or a more powerful and fast car. More than one million Mustangs were sold in only two years. The Mustang is still being sold today and is still very popular. It was so popular and so different when it was introduced that the Mustang and other cars from other makers which looked like the Mustang were given the name "ponycar." [throws the ball from hand to show BLA] mhm, how the eyes opened and closed. He fell backwards and then he did XXX. Title: How to Decorate a Jar with Tissue Paper **Things You'll Need:** 1. A clean glass jar 2. Tissue paper in various colors and patterns 3. Mod Podge or any decoupage glue 4. Foam brush or paintbrush 5. Scissors 6. Pencil 7. Ruler (optional) 8. Clear spray sealant (optional) **Step 1: Clean Your Jar Thoroughly** Before you start decorating your jar, make sure it is free from dust and dirt. Use warm soapy water to wash the jar, then let it dry completely before moving on to the next step. This ensures that the adhesive will stick properly to the surface of the jar. **Step 2: Measure and Cut Out Your Design** Decide on the design you want for your jar. It could be simple stripes, polka dots, flowers, or an abstract pattern - whatever suits your fancy! Using a pencil, lightly trace this design onto the back side of your tissue paper. If you're doing straight lines like stripes, using a ruler can help keep them even. After tracing, cut out your designs carefully with scissors. **Key Tips:** - Keep the pieces large enough so they won't tear when handling later. - Consider how the different elements of your design will fit together on the curved surface of the jar. **Step 3: Apply Decoupage Glue Onto The Jar** Pour some Mod Podge into a disposable container or directly onto a paper plate if you prefer. Dip your foam brush or paintbrush into the glue and spread a thin layer over the area where you plan to place your first piece of tissue paper. Be generous but avoid making it too thick as it might cause bubbles to form under the tissue paper. **Step 4: Place The First Piece of Tissue Paper** Gently lay your pre-cut tissue paper onto the wet glued section of the jar. Smooth down the tissue paper starting from the center and working your way outwards to remove any air bubbles. Press firmly but gently to ensure good contact between the glue and the tissue paper. **Step 5: Repeat Steps 3 & 4** Continue applying the glue and placing tissue paper until your entire jar is covered. Overlap the edges slightly to prevent gaps showing through. Allow each section to dry before adding another layer on top to reduce wrinkling. **Step 6: Seal With Another Layer of Decoupage Glue** Once all your tissue paper is stuck down, go over the whole jar again with another coat of Mod Podge. This seals everything in place and gives a nice finish. Let it dry according to the manufacturer's instructions, usually around 20-30 minutes. **Step 7: Optional Clear Spray Sealant** For added durability, especially if the jar will be used regularly or handled often, consider applying a clear spray sealant once the Mod Podge has fully dried. This adds an extra layer of protection against wear and tear. And there you have it! A beautifully decorated jar made by yourself. Fill it up with candies, cookies, or anything else you desire. Enjoy your crafty creation! On his journey home, Odysseus lands on the island of the Cyclops Polyphemus. They go inside his cave at the sight of sheep, cheese and milk. The Cyclops prays to his father Poseidon that Odysseus would not get home to Ithaca and if he was fated to make it home, Odysseus would lose all of his men, his ship and find trouble at home. This prayer becomes the plot line of the Odyssey. The wombats of Australia fall into two main groups, common wombats and hairy-nosed wombats. The two types look very similar, apart from the hairiness of their noses. However, they are adapted to different environments. Common wombats live in forested areas and eat mostly green food with lots of moisture. They often feed in the daytime. Hairy-nosed wombats live on hot dry plains where they eat dry grass with very little water or nutrition in it. Their metabolic rate is slow and they sleep most of the day underground. Fungi use spores to survive and spread to new sources of food. Spores are not seeds. A seed contains a small form of a plant, plus some food to help it get started, wrapped in a hard shell. A seed is made of Most spores are single cells protected by a cell wall. Some spores are made of several cells, but the largest spore is still smaller than the smallest seed (made by orchids). There is no microscopic fungus inside a spore. A spore contains all the chemicals needed to make its kind of fungus. When conditions are right, the spore starts to grow and creates a web-like mycelium, the fungus individual. In this activity, you will be taking apart a clock to find out how it works. Have you ever wondered how a clock functions? How the hands turn and why the clock will sometimes just stop? In this project you will get to take a clock apart to discover all of the intricate pieces that go into making the clock function. Break up into small groups: - One clock per group - Open the back of the clock - Diagram the inside of the clock Create a flowchart that represents the process that takes place inside the clock as the gears turn. In the flowchart, show how the gear position changes with each minute and hour that passes. Also, show the relationship between gear movement and the hands on the clock face. Share your flowchart with the rest of the class and discuss what is happening inside the clock. It will take one class period to examine the clock and make a flowchart and a second class period to finish the flowchart and present it to the class. Not only will we show our geometrical skill, but we satisfy a requirement of logic: We will prove that these "right angles" that we have defined actually exist. For a definition is required only to be understood. We may not assume that what we have defined exists. Thus, to draw a straight line at right angles to the straight line AB from the given point C, place the point of the compass at C, and mark off equal distances at D and E. With D as center and radius DE, draw an arc. With E as center and with the same radius, draw an arc, and call their point of intersection F. Then CF will be perpendicular to AB at C. Do you see why? Here is the proof. PROPOSITION 11. PROBLEM Using reductio ad absurdam we can prove: Only one line can be drawn perpendicular to a given straight line For if we suppose that CF and CG are both perpendicular to AB, then angle ACF is equal to angle ACG (Postulate 4), the smaller to the larger; which is absurd. Dropping a perpendicular Here is the problem of "dropping" a perpendicular: To a given straight line that may be made as long as we please, Can you solve this problem? Can you draw a perpendicular line from C to AB? To see the answer, pass your mouse over the colored area. Place the point of the compass at C, and with a radius greater than the distance of C to AB, draw arcs at F and G. Now bisect the straight line FG at H. That is, with F as center and radius FG, draw arcs. With G as center and that same radius, draw arcs intersecting those at K and L. Then the straight line KCHL will be the perpendicular bisector of FG. Here is the proof. PROPOSITION 12. PROBLEM The lines CF, CG that we draw are called auxiliary lines -- helping lines. They allow us to make triangles and hence see the proof. A word about what we mean by a given line or a given point, because those expressions occur in many propositions. For something to be given in this science, we must in some way be able to recognize it or know it. "That one." A line will be given if we can reproduce it. For a point to be given, we must be able to reproduce its position. "There." This is analogous to the idea of a given number, which is a number we can recognize by its name; and which, if it is not rational, we can approximate by a rational number as closely as we please. The requirement of given points, lines, and numbers insures that we do not lose touch with what we actually know. Please "turn" the page and do some Problems. Continue on to the next proposition. Tell them you will command them to do certain movements. If you don t say "Simon says" before the command, the class should not do the action. - Say the following (see below for more): - "Simon Says: Sizzle like cooking vegetables." Encourage students to wiggle around. - "Simon Says: Twirl like pasta." Encourage them to twirl around. - "Simon Says: Stretch like cheese." Encourage them to raise their hands high up in the air. - "Simon Says: Swim like a fish." Encourage them to wiggle as if moving through water. - "Be round like a potato." - "Simon says: Be stiff like a carrot stick." Encourage them to stand stiff and straight. - "Simon Says: Wrap yourself up like a wrap!" Encourage students to hug themselves. - "Be small like a grain of rice." Children should be encouraged to consume a variety of nutrient-rich foods low in fat and added sugar for dinner. There are five food groupings: - milk and milk products - meats, beans, and nuts Children should eat foods from all five groupings everyday. More Simon Says Actions: - "Simon Says: Flow like milk." There are three main strategic differences between earlier parts of the game and endgame: Once there was an infant playing in a garden. He was with his mom and dad. What are you going to do with it? D' you want a piece of cake? Title: Understanding Sine and Cosine Graphs Hello young mathematicians! Today, we're going to learn about two special types of graphs called "sine" and "cosine." These graphs have unique shapes and properties that make them really interesting to study. Let's dive into it! First, take a look at these two graphs: (Draw or display images of a sine wave with 5 turning points and a cosine wave with 4 turning points.) The blue graph represents the function $y = \text{sin} \; x$, while the orange graph shows us the function $y = \text{cos} \; x$. You may notice that they look similar but not exactly the same. They have different numbers of peaks and valleys, which we call "turning points." Now let's answer some questions based on our graphs: 1. **What is the period for $y = \text{sin} \; x$ and $y = \text{cos} \; x$?** Imagine drawing each graph on rubber sheets and then stretching or shrinking them horizontally until they match their original positions. The distance you would stretch them without changing their shape is called the *period*. In this case, both sine and cosine have a period of $2\pi$ (two times pi). It means that after every $2\pi$ units along the horizontal axis, the graph repeats itself. 2. **What is the amplitude for $y = \text{sin} \; x$ and $y = \text{cos} \; x$?** Have you ever been on a swing? When you go higher, your swings become longer, and when you don't rise too high, your swings stay close to the ground. Similarly, the *amplitude* of a graph tells us how tall its peaks and deep its valleys are compared to the center line ($y=0$). Both sine and cosine have an amplitude of 1 because their highest point reaches +1 and their lowest point goes down to -1 regarding the center line. 3. **What are the intercepts for $y = \text{sin} \; x$?** An *intercept* occurs when a graph crosses the vertical or horizontal axis. Since sine starts at zero and returns to zero between each pair of peaks, there are infinitely many intercepts spaced evenly along the horizontal axis. To locate them precisely, find the values where $x = n \times 2\pi$ for any integer value of $n$. 4. **What are the intercepts for $y = \text{cos} \; x$?** Cosine begins at its maximum value (+1) instead of starting at zero like sine does; hence, its first intercept happens later than the sine's initial intercept. However, just like sine, cosine also repeatedly crosses the horizontal axis. Its intercepts occur at $x = (2n+1)\frac{\pi}{2}$ for all integers $n$. In summary, sine and cosine are essential building blocks in mathematics, particularly in trigonometry. Remembering their basic properties - periods, amplitudes, and intercepts - sets up a strong foundation for understanding more complex concepts as you grow older! Keep exploring, keep learning, and most importantly, have fun doing math! I'm not one of those psycho moms, but I'm just yeah. Here get your hand out and clap. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to get the Septagon symbol by your gamertag in Halo 3: **Step 1: Reach Level 52** The first step to getting the Septagon symbol is to reach level 52 in Halo 3. This may take some time, but there are several ways to speed up the process. Playing multiplayer matches will earn you experience points (XP) that will help you rank up faster than playing campaign missions alone. Completing challenges and commendations can also give you bonus XP. Key tip: Try to play objective-based games like Capture the Flag or King of the Hill, as these modes tend to grant more XP compared to other match types. **Step 2: Unlock the Skull Multiplier** At level 52, you unlock the "Black Eye" skull. Equip this skull before starting any matchmaking game. The Black Eye skull doubles the amount of XP earned from kills when your shields are depleted. It's essential to use this skull during Step 3 since it significantly boosts the rate at which you accumulate XP. Guideline: Make sure to enable the skull under the "Options" menu before joining a matchmaking game. **Step 3: Grind Matchmaking Games** Now that you have the Black Eye skull equipped, join matchmaking games and focus on racking up kills. Since your shields need to be down for the Black Eye skull to work, try using weapons with quick recharge times such as plasma pistols or SMGs. You can switch back to your preferred weapon once the kill has been made. Keep track of your total XP gained after every match and aim to hit one million XP. Key tip: If possible, find a group of friends who share the same goal so that you all can contribute towards achieving the required XP together. Teamwork increases efficiency! **Step 4: Achieve One Million XP** Once you reach one million XP post-level 52, you should receive a notification informing you about acquiring the prestigious Septagon symbol next to your gamertag. Congratulations! At this point, you might want to consider resetting your stats if you wish to repeat the grind again for additional symbols. Guidelines: * Be patient; grinding XP takes time and commitment. * Remember to take breaks regularly to avoid burnout. * Always prioritize having fun while playing over focusing solely on earning XP. By following these steps meticulously, you'll soon sport the coveted Septagon symbol beside your gamertag in Halo 3. Good luck and happy gaming! I packed him out from Luxlow along with the grub. In recent years, overfishing caused a decline in lake trout. The drop in lake trout increased the alewife population. In response, the government introduced salmon as a predator to decrease the alewife population. This program was so successful that the salmon population rose rapidly, and the states surrounding Lake Michigan promoted 'salmon snagging'. Plants: Putting Down Roots Scientists say at least 300,000 different types of plants exist today. Plants provide us with food, shelter, and even clothing. Nearly all plants live on land. They hold themselves in place with roots. Roots also absorb water from the soil. Almost all plants make their own food through photosynthesis. Parts of the plant contain a green substance called chlorophyll. These parts combine carbon dioxide and water in the presence of sunlight to make sugar and release oxygen. Not all plants make seeds. Walk through a forest, and you'll see moss growing on trees or rocks. The 10,000 species of moss are a type of plant called bryophyte. They reproduce by making spores, similar to fungi. Seedless vascular plants also make spores. Vascular means the plant has "tubing." The tubing gets water from the roots to the leaves where the plant makes food. The 12,000 species of ferns are seedless vascular plants. Other plants reproduce from seeds. Many, but not all, seed-bearing plants make their seeds in flowers. This week we had fun practicing our measuring skills. No books required. Just a few supplies that we happened to have on hand in the house. - marbles (preferably one small and one large), - extra large mailing tube, - measuring tape (or ruler), - blocks/books/VHS cassettes for propping up one end of the tube, - a pencil and paper for recording your results. To learn a little bit about math and physics, our goal was to record how far each marble traveled each time we raised one end of the tube. We started out by laying the tube flat on the ground and placing a marble in one end. This was our control. Then we raised it one inch at a time, and again placed the marble in the opening of the raised end. Each time the marble rolled out the other side, we measured how far from the tube it rolled before coming to a stop. After we had performed our "experiment" 8 times, we changed out our small marble for a larger one and did the same thing, recording the results. Afterward, I showed my kids how to make a line graph using the data we had collected. Here's what our results looked like: Ways to extend the learning: - Before you begin the experiment, ask your kids what they think the marble will do if you raise one end of the tube. - Before using the second marble (bigger or smaller, it doesn't matter), ask your kids if they think it will roll farther or not as far as the first marble. - Afterward, ask your child too look at the results and tell you which one rolled the furthest. Then ask them why that happened. - You can continue this experiment until the mailing tube is perfectly vertical and see how this affects how far the marbles will roll.
