Title: How to Address a Commissioner Introduction: In both professional and social settings, addressing someone with respect and accuracy is essential for effective communication and building positive relationships. This article will provide you with an in-depth guide on how to address a commissioner, including key tips and guidelines to ensure clarity and practicality. By following these steps, you'll be able to confidently engage in conversations while demonstrating your understanding of proper etiquette. Step 1: Identify the Type of Commissioner Commissioners can hold various positions across different fields, such as politics, sports, or business. To properly address them, determine their specific role first. Common types include city commissioners, county commissioners, park commissioners, or sports league commissioners. Knowing this information allows you to understand their authority and responsibilities, which will help tailor your language when speaking or writing to them. Step 2: Formal Titles vs. First Names When referring to a commissioner in written correspondence (e.g., letters, emails), always begin by using their formal title followed by their last name. For instance, if you are addressing a letter to Jane Smith, who serves as the Parks & Recreation Commissioner, write "Dear Commissioner Smith" at the beginning of the message. Using formal titles shows that you acknowledge their position and regard it with respect. Key Tip: Avoid abbreviated forms like "Mr." or "Ms." before their title unless they have specifically requested otherwise. Stick to using just their professional title ("Commissioner") instead. Step 3: Salutation in Spoken Communication In face-to-face interactions or phone calls, introduce yourself politely and then address the commissioner using their appropriate title and last name. For example, say "Good morning, Commissioner Johnson. I am John Doe from XYZ Organization," when meeting a new commissioner for the first time. Guiding Principle: Always maintain a polite and professional tone during spoken communications, regardless of whether the conversation takes place in person, over the phone, or via video conferencing platforms. Step 4: Group Settings and Introductions If introducing a commissioner to others in group situations, briefly explain their role and title first, followed by their last name. Say something along the lines of "I would like to introduce our esteemed City Commissioner, Mr./Ms. Brown." Afterward, allow the commissioner to take charge of the conversation and offer any additional details about themselves if needed. Step 5: Use Appropriate Pronouns Once introduced or engaged in conversation, refer to the commissioner using pronouns consistent with their gender identity. If unsure, ask discretely for clarification or simply stick to using their official title throughout the interaction. Step 6: Referencing Previous Interactions When discussing past encounters or projects involving the commissioner, use phrases like "our previous collaboration" or "during our initial discussion" rather than making assumptions based on personal knowledge about the individual outside their professional capacity. Maintaining a focus on their public duties promotes respect and maintains boundaries between personal and professional spheres. Conclusion: By adhering to these guidelines, you demonstrate respect towards commissioners and establish trustworthy connections within your community. Properly addressing commissioners also showcases your awareness of professional norms and fosters positive impressions among colleagues, constituents, and peers alike. Remember, taking the time to learn and implement appropriate protocol ensures smooth communication channels and enhances overall interpersonal relations. oh, I know Laurie told me but let's see if it's what Laurie told me. People who are born in American Samoa are called U.S. nationals, not U.S. citizens. This means they have some of the same rights as citizens but not all of them. Unlike people who are not Americans at all, American Samoans can go to any other part of the United States they want and live there for as long as they want. But they are not allowed to vote, to be on juries in court, to run for office, or to hold any job that the employee has to be a citizen to hold. For contrast, someone born in Puerto Rico or Guam is a citizen, so if they move to a state, they are allowed to vote in that state and in all federal elections. The government of the United Kingdom was also pro-Pinochet. = = = children/CHIDES_NA/Gilliam/TD/5m/46906ca.cha = = = How to Cope When Someone You Look Up To Ignores You Online Introduction: In today's digital age, it is common to look up to people online who inspire us or have achieved success in our areas of interest. However, there may be instances where these individuals ignore our attempts at communication. This can lead to feelings of disappointment, hurt, and even rejection. The following steps will guide you through coping with such situations while maintaining your self-esteem and mental wellbeing. Step 1: Acknowledge Your Feelings Recognize that it is natural to feel upset when someone we admire ignores us. It is essential to acknowledge these emotions instead of suppressing them. Allow yourself time to process what has happened before moving forward. By acknowledging your feelings, you validate your experiences and create space for healing. Key tip: Remember that experiencing negative emotions does not reflect weakness but rather shows vulnerability - an integral part of being human. Step 2: Reflect On The Situation Analyze the context surrounding the incident objectively. Consider factors like their busy schedule, platform etiquette, or miscommunication as possible reasons behind their lack of response. Try putting yourself in their shoes; this perspective shift might help explain why they ignored you. Guideline: Avoid jumping to conclusions about their intentions without sufficient evidence. Step 3: Manage Expectations Reevaluate your expectations from this person. Understand that celebrities, influencers, or professionals often receive numerous messages daily and cannot respond personally to everyone. Moreover, just because you admire someone doesn't guarantee mutual interaction or friendship. Key tip: Focus on building genuine connections with those around you instead of seeking validation from distant figures. Step 4: Practice Self-Compassion Treat yourself kindly during this challenging time. Engage in activities that bring joy and comfort. Speak to yourself as if addressing a close friend - using gentle language devoid of harsh criticism. Maintaining self-compassion fosters resilience amidst adversities and enhances overall emotional health. Guideline: Implement mindfulness practices like meditation, deep breathing exercises, or progressive muscle relaxation to alleviate stress. Step 5: Seek Support If Needed If the situation significantly impacts your mood or disrupts daily functioning, consider reaching out to trusted friends, family members, or mental health professionals for support. Sharing your concerns with others provides valuable insights, encouragement, and reassurance. Key tip: Recall past experiences where you successfully navigated similar challenges; drawing upon previous triumphs bolsters confidence in overcoming current obstacles. Step 6: Foster Resilience Develop strategies to cope with future occurrences of feeling overlooked by role models or peers online. Build a strong sense of identity based on intrinsic values and strengths rather than external validation. Additionally, expand your network of supportive relationships both offline and online to minimize reliance on any single individual for affirmation. Guideline: Regularly engage in hobbies, interests, and personal growth opportunities to nurture independence and self-reliance. Conclusion: Being ignored by someone we look up to online can evoke potent emotions. Following these steps enables coping mechanisms that promote emotional stability, foster resilience, and cultivate healthy attitudes towards virtual interactions. Ultimately, remember that one's worth should never be contingent upon another person's attention or approval. The same voice she heard that said this was Michelle, Sarah's friend. Craft activities (ages 6-10) Children like making things, so craft activities are a great way for you and your child to have fun practising English. To give your child an extra reason for the craft activity, there are games you can play with the things you make. Keep everything in a box so you can play the games again. You will use a lot of the same language for each of these activities, for example: What colour is this? Where is the red pen? Where are the scissors? Draw / Write here. How do you spell...? Colour it in. Let your child hear you use this language and sometimes let them be teacher, so they have to tell you what to do. Cut out pictures of food from magazines. Use the pictures to make a food face. When you are making the faces, ask your child, e.g. What's this? Is this a nose? Your child can talk or write about the finished face, e.g. The mouth is a banana. The left eye is a grape. Make simple hand puppets. If your child makes two different colour bird puppets, they can use them when they say the Little birds rhyme from the Action games section This is a sheet of helpful language (PDF, 200KB) from the video. Also included in Video 6 Make a clock to practise telling the time. Use: - a circle of card or a paper plate; - card for the clock hands; - a split pin to hold the clock hands in place. Help your child to mark out where the numbers go. You can ask your child to write the number word under each number to give them practice with these spellings. Use the split pin to hold the hands in place. You can see a finished clock on the video. When the clock is finished, say some times for your child to put on the clock (check which times they have learned in their English course book), e.g. Five o'clock; half past six; quarter past nine; quarter to twelve. Make a board game. You or your child can draw out a grid on some paper. Make the squares big so you can write in them. Mark the 'Start' and the 'Finish'. Write instructions in some of the squares, e.g. Miss a turn. Go back one place. Go forward two places. You can also have some cards with pictures of familiar words, e.g. animals, food or colours. [Adam and Ursula are alone in the room. Adam is serving drinks from the toy water cooler] Let's imagine we are living in a world made up of only two colors, red and blue. Everyone wants to paint their houses either red or blue, but here's the twist - no two neighboring houses can have the same color! This is like creating a checkerboard pattern with red and blue squares alternating. Now let's think about taking a picture of our colored neighborhood from different viewpoints. When we stand far away, things start looking differently than when we are up close. From really far away, we may not even see individual houses anymore; instead, we just see large patches of red and blue. In mathematics, there is a concept called "projective spaces," which is related to these different ways of viewing our neighborhood. In particular, real projective spaces behave like those "large patches of color" that appear when we step back and look at the big picture. On the other hand, spheres act more like the surface of the Earth, representing all possible directions we could point at while standing in one spot. The problem mentioned earlier asks us to create a connection between these two concepts using something called a quotient map. It combines every pair of opposite points on a sphere into one single point in the real projective space, just like merging together each pair of identical-colored houses in our neighborhood example. To make sure everything works out correctly, we want to show that both forward and backward mappings preserve important properties, i.e., they should be continuous functions. To keep things simple for grade schoolers, let's focus on explaining continuity through our neighborhood analogy. A continuous mapping means that if we slightly change our position (move a bit closer or further), the colors we see don't suddenly jump from red to blue or vice versa. Instead, they gradually transition between shades of red and blue. Similarly, when working with mathematical objects, continuity ensures smooth transitions without any sudden jumps. So now, our goal is to prove that the backward mapping (from real projective space to spherical patches) preserves this property of gradual changes, ensuring that it is also a continuous function. We will do this using an approach similar to showing continuity for the forward direction. Although it might seem challenging initially, understanding the basics behind this idea helps build strong foundations for exploring complex math concepts later on. The Power of 2 This is a level 5 algebra strand activity from the Figure It Out series. find a rule to describe a non linear number pattern In this activity, the students should initially use geoboards or square dot paper to help them work out the area of the striped squares. The area of the striped square in figure 1 (which is the shape in iv) is the area of the surrounding 4 by 4 square, that is, 16 square units, minus the area of the four corner triangles. The area of each triangle is 2 square units (one-half of a 2 by 2 square). So the striped area is equal to 16 square units minus 4 x 2 square units. We usually write 8 square units as 8 units2, so in this case, the area is 16 units2 - 8 units2 = 8 units2. The area of the striped square in figure 2 (which is the shape in iii) is the area of the striped square from figure 1 minus the area of the four corner triangles. The area of each of these triangles is 1 unit2 (two halves of a 1 by 1 square). So the striped area in figure 2 is equal to 8 units2 - 4 x 1 = 4 units2. The students need to repeat this process for the other striped squares and write a rule connecting successive striped square areas. They will see that the striped area in figure 1 is double the striped area in figure 2. This relationship can also be seen clearly by folding squares of paper, as illustrated in the diagram below. Square A is twice the area of square B. Square A is the square that encloses square B. So a simple rule is: the area of a square is twice the area of the square it encloses or, as given in the Answers, the area of a square is double the area of the enclosed square. In this activity, the students repeatedly fold and then cut pieces of paper. Each cut doubles the number of pieces of paper. These results can be shown in a table: So, for 10 cuts, there will be 210 pieces of paper. This is 2 x 2 x 2 x 2 x 2 x 2 x 2 x 2 x 2 x 2 = 1 024. We express this as "2 to the power of 10 equals 1 024". Pressing the following sequence of keys on most calculators will give the value of 210 as 1 024. Where this sequence of keys doesn't work, experiment until you find a sequence that does. For example, the following sequence works with some calculators: Scientific calculators can also be used to find the value of powers of numbers. We can express these algebraically as yx, which we say as "any number, y, raised to the power of any number, x". So the value of 57 can be found by pressing the following keys: Note that some scientific calculators use the key xy instead of yx. This activity explores a simplified version of a mathematical puzzle that was invented in 1883 by the French mathematician Edouard Lucas (1842-1891). The puzzle is known as the Tower of Hanoi and sometimes as the Tower of Brahma. It was inspired by a Hindu legend that tells of the mental discipline demanded of young priests. The legend says that at the beginning of time, the priests in the temple were given 64 gold discs, each one a little smaller than the one beneath it. The priests were to transfer the 64 gold discs from one of three poles to another, via the second pole where necessary, in such a way that a disc could never be placed on top of a smaller disc. The legend goes on to say that when this task was finished, the temple would crumble into dust and the world would end. The students are initially asked to see if they can work out the minimum number of moves to transfer only 3 lids (discs) rather than the 64 in the legend. To understand how this puzzle works, the students will find it helpful to try the puzzle with just 1 lid and then 2 lids. The following diagrams show the moves for 1, 2, and 3 lids. If you look closely at the diagrams, you may notice a symmetrical pattern. Each diagram has a centre of rotational symmetry, marked with a . The pattern for the number of moves is shown in this table: The pattern in the table shows that there are 2 If we return to the Hindu legend associated with this puzzle, we see that to transfer 64 gold discs, 264 - 1 moves will be required. Altogether, this is 18 446 744 073 709 551 615 moves. If the priests worked continuously for 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, making one move every second, the complete transfer would take slightly more than 580 billion years. This is more than current estimates for the age of the universe, so the legend may well be correct in asserting that the world will end when the priests finish their task! This is the force that acts on the body in a direction away from the centre, which contributes to making the body try to fly away. Tabbed browsing is a feature used in the browser where many webpages can be opened in one window, with labeled tabs on top to select the webpage. To make a new empty tab, type control-T. continental glacier is a huge mass of ice that covers a lot of land near the Arctic or Antarctic polar regions. Continental glaciers are found in and around the Arctic, Antarctic circles, Greenland and islands in the polar regions. These huge glaciers are nothing more than huge chunks of ice that began with a single snowfall. First a layer of snow falls in one area. After a while more snow falls until the layer is thicker. Each time a new layer of snow falls it adds weight to the glacier. The layers below the top layer begin to squeeze together until most of air is squeezed out from between the particles of snow. At this point ice forms from the crushed snow. So, the lower ice layers in a glacier are thinner and denser than the layers toward the top of the glacier. This process has gone on for millions of years. the Arctic circle continental glaciers are mostly built on top of frozen sea water, but the glaciers do spread out onto the land that it touches. Year after year the ice and snow pile up until it weighs millions of tons. Some continental glaciers are nearly one mile deep. Over time the lowest layers of the glacier melt a little bit. Gravity will pull the glacier down any slope on the slippery layer of ice at the bottom of the glacier. Cedarville was forced to kick. Look the baby has a shirt on. The year 2011 has some unusual dates ... 1/1/11, 1/11/11, 11/1/11, Take the last 2 digits of the year in which you were born. Then add it to the age that you will be in 2011. What do you get? Yes, the sum will always equal 111 (if you were born in the Twentieth Century). So, why does this work? Let x = last 2 digits of the year in which you were born. If you were born in the Twentieth Century, Then your age this year will be 11 + (100 - x). The 11 represents the number of years from 2000 to 2011. The quantity (100 - x) is your age as of the year 2000. So, when you add the two quantities together, you obtain: x + (11 + (100 - x)), which equals 111. Kim hit the balloon, and it went up in the air, huh? The Keel Billed Toucan ("Ramphastos sulfuratus") is the National Bird of Belize. It is noted for its great, canoe-shaped bill and its brightly coloured green, blue, red and orange feathers. There are toucans in open areas of the country with large trees. It is mostly black with bright yellow cheeks and chest, red under the tail and a distinctive white patch at the base of the tail. They make a monotonous frog-like croak. Toucans like fruits. They eat by cutting with the serrated edge of their bills. How does propulsion work? Mass is important because of two major factors affecting how things move in space: inertia and gravity. The more mass something has, the more of both it experiences. That is why heavy things (things with a lot of mass) are hard to move. When an object is sitting still, it resists moving, and the more mass it has the more it resists. The amount of thrust needed to move something and how fast it ends up moving are both directly tied to its mass. On the other hand, once something massive starts moving, it is very hard to stop. This is also due to the relationship between mass and inertia. Gravity is also proportional to how much mass each thing has. The bigger an object is, the larger the gravitational pull it exerts. Because of gravity and inertia, the more massive something is, the harder it is to get into space, the harder it is to keep it there, and the harder it is to move it where you want it to go when it is there. For that reason, one of the goals of the New Millennium project is to make lightweight spacecraft. How did DS1 get into space? How do objects in space travel? How does gravity work in space? What is the new Millennium project? What is thrust? What is inertia? What is escape velocity? What is mass? Why do mass and distance affect gravity? And so then they had a problem and stuff. Title: Cope With a Difficult Eighteen-Year-Old Daughter Introduction: Adolescence is a challenging time for both parents and children. As your daughter transitions into adulthood, she may exhibit difficult behaviors as she seeks independence and establishes her own identity. This guide will provide you with steps and strategies to help you cope with an eighteen-year-old daughter who is going through a tough phase. Remember that every individual is unique, so adapt these suggestions to fit your specific situation. Step 1: Maintain Open Communication * Explanation: Clear communication fosters understanding and strengthens relationships. By maintaining open lines of communication, you can better understand your daughter's thoughts, feelings, and motivations, which can lead to more effective problem-solving and conflict resolution. * Key Tips: + Listen actively by giving her undivided attention, asking questions, and summarizing what she said to ensure mutual understanding. + Speak calmly and respectfully, even when disagreements arise. Avoid criticism or judgment, as this may cause her to shut down and become less willing to share her thoughts and feelings. + Be patient; allow her time to express herself without interrupting or finishing her sentences. + Validate her emotions by acknowledging her feelings and showing empathy. Step 2: Set Boundaries and Establish Expectations * Explanation: Clearly defined boundaries create structure and promote healthy relationships. Setting expectations and consequences encourages responsibility, accountability, and personal growth. * Key Guidelines: + Collaborate with your daughter to establish reasonable household rules and expectations regarding behavior, chores, curfews, etc. They're so many you're afraid they're going to fall off? So, one day he decided to go look for a special drink which would help to make his whistling even more perfect. He searched and searched, but he couldn't find anything! But then he came across a pond and he saw something sparkling in the water. He bent down and he scooped it up in his beak - it was the perfect drink! It was cold and sweet and it made him feel amazing. He drank it in one big gulp and then he started to whistle. But this time his whistling was even better than before - it was perfect! The duck was so happy that he danced around the pond, flapping his wings. He was so proud that he had found the special drink. Ole Christensen R mer used an astronomical measurement to make the first quantitative estimate of the speed of light. When measured from Earth, the periods of moons orbiting a distant planet are shorter when the Earth is approaching the planet than when the Earth is receding from it. The distance travelled by light from the planet (or its moon) to Earth is shorter when the Earth is at the point in its orbit that is closest to its planet than when the Earth is at the farthest point in its orbit, the difference in distance being the diameter of the Earth's orbit around the Sun. The observed change in the moon's orbital period is actually the difference in the time it takes light to traverse the shorter or longer distance. R mer observed this effect for Jupiter's innermost moon Io, and he deduced that light takes 22 minutes to cross the diameter of the Earth's orbit. II The Early Heroes The oldest Roman stories give a description of the coming of the people who afterwards inhabited the city, from across the seas. They tell of the founding of the first township round the Seven Hills, and of the kings, especially of the last seven, who ruled over the people until, for their misdeeds, they were driven out and the very name of King became hateful in Roman ears. Then there are many tales of the wars between the people of Rome and the neighbours dwelling round them on the plains of Latium and among the hills of Etruria and Samnium; and the fierce battles fought against the Gauls who, from time to time, swept down on Italy from the mountains of the north. Saudi Arabia officially known as the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia , is a country in Western Asia including most of the Arabian Peninsula. The land area is approximately , so Saudi Arabia is geographically the 5th-largest state in Asia and 2nd-largest state in the Arab world after Algeria. Saudi Arabia is bordered by Jordan and Iraq to the north, Kuwait to the northeast, Qatar, Bahrain, and the United Arab Emirates to the east, Oman to the southeast, and Yemen to the south. It is separated from Israel and Egypt by the Gulf of Aqaba. It is the only nation with both a Red Sea coast and a Persian Gulf coast, and most of its land is dry and barren. Saudi Arabia is the birthplace of Arabs and Islam and sometimes called "the Land of the Two Holy Mosques" in reference to Al-Masjid al-Haram (in Mecca), and Al-Masjid an-Nabawi (in Medina), the two holiest places in Islam. Arabic is the exclusive official language in Saudi Arabia. Within these regions, there are up to 48 independent European countries (with the identities of 5 transcontinental countries being disputed). The largest is the Russian Federation, which covers 39% of Europe. Come on tell me about the story why you shouldn't scream in someone ear. Photosynthesis is the process by which plants and some microorganisms make substances like carbohydrates. It is an endothermic (takes in heat) chemical process that uses sunlight to turn carbon dioxide into sugars. The sugars are used by the cell as energy, and to build other kinds of molecules. Title: How to Deal with Annoying Relatives Introduction: Dealing with annoying relatives can be challenging, especially during family gatherings or holidays. However, there are effective strategies you can employ to maintain your sanity and enjoy these occasions. This guide will provide you with detailed steps on how to deal with difficult relatives while preserving familial relationships. Step 1: Identify the Issue Determine what makes this relative annoying. Is it their constant criticism, nosiness, or negative attitude? Understanding the root cause allows you to address the problem more effectively. Key Tip: Remember that people's behaviors often stem from their own insecurities and issues, not necessarily directed at you personally. Step 2: Set Boundaries Once you know why they annoy you, set clear boundaries about acceptable behavior. Let them know gently but firmly when they cross those lines. For instance, if they always criticize your life choices, say something like, "I appreciate your concern, but I would prefer if we could discuss other topics." Guideline: Be respectful yet assertive; do not let others trample over your feelings and space. Step 3: Stay Calm and Collected It's crucial to keep your cool even when provoked. Responding angrily might escalate the situation. Instead, take deep breaths, count to ten, or remove yourself from the conversation temporarily until you regain composure. Key Tip: Practice mindfulness techniques to help manage stress and remain calm in tense situations. Step 4: Avoid Controversial Topics Steer clear of subjects likely to spark disagreements, such as politics, religion, or personal finances. If the topic veers towards one of these areas, diplomatically change the subject. You could also suggest playing a game or watching a movie together instead of engaging in heated debates. Step 5: Show Empathy Try to understand where your relative is coming from. They may have had a tough day, week, or year. Their irritating behavior might just be a manifestation of their internal struggles. By showing empathy, you demonstrate maturity and kindness, which could de-escalate tension. Step 6: Seek Allies Find allies among your relatives who share similar sentiments about the bothersome relative. Together, strategize ways to handle uncomfortable situations or distract the person causing distress. Just knowing you're not alone can make dealing with the situation easier. Step 7: Limit Interaction Time If possible, reduce the amount of time spent around the annoying relative. Arrive late to events or leave early to minimize contact. Alternatively, position yourself away from them during meals or activities. Step 8: Practice Positive Reinforcement Reward good behavior by acknowledging it. Compliment your relative when they behave well or contribute positively to the gathering. Over time, they may associate positive interactions with you, leading to improved relations. Step 9: Consider Professional Help If the relationship becomes too toxic or harmful, consider seeking professional assistance. Therapists specializing in family dynamics can offer valuable insights and guidance on managing strained relationships. Conclusion: Remember, dealing with annoying relatives requires patience, understanding, and resilience. It's essential to protect your mental health without compromising important familial bonds. Implementing these steps consistently should lead to smoother interactions and less stressful family gatherings. This is the crooked long neck one. And then you can play with the baby dolls. Title: Get Creative With Your Bridal Gifts ========================================= Introduction: Making a bridal gift special and memorable can be challenging but also incredibly rewarding. By getting creative with your present, you're showing that you put thought, time, and effort into selecting or making something unique and meaningful for the happy couple. This wiki-style guide will walk you through various ways to make your bridal gift stand out from the rest. Table of Contents ----------------- * [Step 1: Understand Their Interests](#understand) + Explanation + Key Tips + Guidelines * [Step 2: Choose a Personalized Theme](#theme) + Explanation + Key Tips + Guidelines * [Step 3: Opt for Handmade Gifts](#handmade) + Explanation shh, shh shh don't yell, don't yell. [the kitten is attached to the bottom of the barrel] Title: How to Avoid Acne/Pimples: A Comprehensive Guide Acne is a common skin condition that affects people of all ages. It occurs when hair follicles become plugged with oil and dead skin cells, leading to whiteheads, blackheads or pimples. While it's impossible to completely eliminate the risk of acne, there are several steps you can take to minimize its occurrence. This article will provide an in-depth guide on how to avoid acne/pimples. **Step 1: Maintain Good Hygiene** Cleaning your face twice daily with warm water and a gentle cleanser can help remove excess oils, dirt, and bacteria which may contribute to acne breakouts. However, over-washing can irritate your skin and make acne worse. Use your hands rather than a washcloth to prevent skin irritation. Key Tip: Avoid harsh soaps as they can strip essential oils from your skin causing dryness and stimulate more oil production, worsening acne. --- **Step 2: Choose Products Wisely** Opt for non-comedogenic (won't clog pores) skincare products and cosmetics. Look for labels indicating 'oil-free' or 'non-acnegenic'. Heavy foundations and creams should be avoided as they tend to block pores. Guideline: The American Academy of Dermatology recommends removing makeup before bedtime to allow your skin to breathe overnight. --- **Step 3: Moisturize Properly** Moisturizing is crucial even if you have oily skin. Skin without adequate moisture tends to produce extra oil, increasing the likelihood of acne development. Select lightweight, water-based moisturizers suitable for your skin type. Key Tip: If using sunscreen, wait at least 15 minutes after applying it before putting on makeup or going outside. Sunscreens containing zinc oxide or titanium dioxide are less likely to cause acne. --- **Step 4: Limit Touching Your Face** Touching your face unnecessarily transfers germs and dirt onto your skin, potentially causing inflammation and acne formation. Be particularly mindful about resting your chin or cheek against your hand during work or while studying. --- **Step 5: Watch What You Eat** Although no specific diet has been scientifically proven to cure acne, certain foods might trigger flares in some individuals due to hormonal fluctuations. These include dairy products, carbohydrate-rich foods like bread, bagels, chips, etc., and foods high in processed sugars. Guideline: Try eliminating these types of food one by one to see if any particular item aggravates your acne. Remember, everyone's body reacts differently; what triggers acne in one person might not affect another. --- **Step 6: Manage Stress Levels** Stress doesn't directly cause acne but it can exacerbate existing conditions. High stress levels lead to increased cortisol production, promoting sebum (oil) production, thereby encouraging bacterial growth and pore blockages. Key Tips: Practice relaxation techniques such as deep breathing exercises, yoga, meditation, or other activities you find calming. Make sure to get enough sleep too! Title: How to Build Broad shoulders Introduction: Broad shoulders are often associated with strength, confidence, and masculinity. While genetics play a role in determining shoulder width, there are exercises and techniques that can help you build broader shoulders and create the illusion of wider shoulders. This guide will provide a comprehensive plan to help you achieve this goal. It's important to remember that building broad shoulders takes time, consistency, and dedication. Always consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new exercise program. Section 1: Understanding Shoulder Anatomy The shoulder is made up of three main muscles: the anterior deltoid (front), lateral deltoid (side), and posterior deltoid (rear). To build broad shoulders, you need to target all three heads equally. A balanced approach ensures proper development, reduces injury risk, and creates an aesthetically pleasing appearance. Section 2: Exercise Selection To build broad shoulders, incorporate compound movements and isolation exercises into your routine. Compound movements like overhead presses engage multiple muscle groups, while isolation exercises like lateral raises focus specifically on the deltoids. Here are some recommended exercises: 1. Overhead Press (Barbell or Dumbbell): Targets all three deltoid heads. * Start with lighter weights and gradually increase as you gain strength. * Maintain proper form by keeping your core engaged and avoiding arching your back. 2. Lateral Raises (Dumbbells): Primarily targets the side deltoids. * Use a controlled motion and avoid swinging the weight. " What is that I heard her say?" In Hesiod's "Theogony", however, Aphrodite is stated to have risen from sea foam, formed at the spot where Uranos' genitals landed, after Kronos castrated him and tossed them into the sea. Aphrodite's cult was centered on the islands of Cythera and Cyprus, both of which were claimed to be her birthplace. Her main festival was the Aphrodisia, which was celebrated annually every midsummer. The Charites (minor goddesses of grace and splendor) attended to Aphrodite and served as her handmaidens. Aphrodite's symbols include the dolphin, myrtle, rose, dove, sparrow, swan and pearl, and the dove, sparrow and swan were her sacred animals. The goddess Venus is her Roman equivalent. Aphrodite was quite often described as very beautiful, and was used as a point of comparison for female beauty - but just as she was beautiful, she just as smart and wise. Gary told me you went up to the snow the other day. We don't height stay in Utica, but we'd like to. Everything around us has a name. People, animals, places and things have names that we call nouns. Nouns are also called naming words. Mother, father, cow, pig, table, book, school are all 'naming words' or 'nouns'. What are you sitting on? Are you sitting on a 'chair'? Say the word''CHAIR' out aloud. This is its name or 'naming word'. It can not eat and drink or move. It has no life. It is a thing. Whenever you read the word 'CHAIR' you see a picture of a chair, in front of your eyes. You also know that you use the chair when you want to sit. What things can you see around you? Call out their names. These names, of the things around you, are their naming words. There are many people who help us. A 'teacher' helps you to study in school. Say the word 'TEACHER'. Whenever you read this word you know that it is the naming word for the person who helps you to study in school. Think about the people who help you every day and those who help you sometimes. Which animals have you seen? Have you seen a cat or a dog? These are naming words for animals. When you read the word 'CAT' you know what kind of animal it is. Think about the animals that you see in a zoo. The names of the animals that you see are their naming words or nouns. Where do you go to study? Yes! You go to school to study. School is a naming word for a place. Which city do you live in? The name of that city is its naming word or noun. Can you think of more places? The names of all these places are called nouns. There are two systems of group numbers; one using Arabic numerals (1,2,3) and the other using Roman numerals (I, II, III). The Roman numeral names were used in most of the 20th century. In 1990 the International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry (IUPAC) decided to use the new system with Arabic numerals, to replace the two old group systems that used Roman numerals. Title: How to Delete Flappy Bird from Your iOS Device Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through the process of deleting the popular game, Flappy Bird, from your iOS device. Whether you're tired of playing or just want to free up some space on your device, this step-by-step guide will help you get the job done. Step 1: Locate the Flappy Bird App Icon The first step is to find the app icon for Flappy Bird on your home screen or in a folder on your iOS device. This may seem obvious, but it's important to ensure that you have located the correct app before attempting to delete it. Key Tip: If you can't find the app, try using the Spotlight search feature by swiping down on the home screen. Type "Flappy Bird" into the search bar, and the app should appear in the results if it's installed on your device. Step 2: Enter Jiggle Mode Once you've found the Flappy Bird app icon, press and hold your finger on the icon until all the apps begin to jiggle. You'll notice small "x" icons appear at the top left corner of each app. This mode allows you to rearrange apps and delete unwanted ones. Key Guideline: Be careful not to tap the "x" accidentally while trying to enter jiggle mode, as doing so will immediately prompt you to delete the app without any confirmation message. Step 3: Tap the "x" Icon Now that you are in jiggle mode, locate the "x" icon at the top left corner of the Flappy Bird app. Tap this "x" icon to initiate the deletion process. A popup window will appear asking if you are sure you want to delete the app. Step 4: Confirm Deletion In the popup window, confirm that you wish to delete the Flappy Bird app by tapping the red "Delete" button. The app will then be removed from your iOS device along with any saved data associated with the app. Key Tip: Before deleting an app, consider backing up any important data related to the app (such as high scores or game progress) via iCloud or another cloud storage service if available. Once deleted, this information cannot be recovered. Step 5: Exit Jiggle Mode After successfully deleting Flappy Bird, simply press the Home button to exit jiggle mode. All remaining apps will stop jiggling, and their respective icons will return to normal size. Conclusion: You have now successfully deleted Flappy Bird from your iOS device! By following these steps, you can manage your apps more effectively and maintain control over the content stored on your device. Remember always to back up important data before deleting an app, especially when it comes to games where progress might be lost otherwise. They both were getting off of the blimp. A complex number is the sum of a real number and another real number multiplied by i, where i is a square root of -1. Thus it can be written as a + ib where a and b are real. We can add two such numbers by adding their real and imaginary parts separately. Thus (5 + 7i) + (2 - 3i) = 7 + 4i. We subtract them similarly: (5 + 7i) - (2 - 3i) = 3 + 10i. We can multiply them as follows: (5 + 7i) * (2 - 3i) = 10 + (14 - 15)i - 21i2 = 31 - i. To do division you make use of the fact that (a + ib) * (a - ib) = a2 - (ib)2 = a2 + b2. Thus you write . It is common to represent complex numbers by points in the "complex plane". The real part of the complex number (a + ib) is a, its imaginary part is b. We represent it by the point with x coordinate a, and y coordinate b. The x axis is, in this complex plane, called the real axis, and the y axis is the imaginary axis. Numbers on the real axis are ordinary real numbers and numbers on the imaginary axis are imaginary numbers. You can represent a complex number alternatively, by its distance to the origin, usually written as r and called its magnitude, and the angle that a line from it to the origin makes with the x axis at the origin, usually called theta (). To anticipate what we will later see, the relations between these quantities is x and y can be expressed in terms of r and by and the wonderful fact implies that we can write 1. (4 + i) / (3 - 2i). 2. (3 + 3i) * (2 - i). 3. Find r given x = 3, y = 4. 4. Find given x = 3, y = -2. 5. Find given x = -2, y = 4. Up Previous Next " "Then how are we to get it off?" The GOAT, going abroad to feed, shut up her young kid at home, charging him to bolt the door fast, and open it to nobody, till she herself should return. The Wolf, who lay lurking just by, heard this charge given, and soon after came and knocked at the door, counterfeiting the voice of the Goat, and desiring to be admitted. The Kid, looking out of the window and discovering the cheat, bid him go about his business; for however he might imitate a Goat's voice, yet he appeared too much like a Wolf to be trusted. MORAL. Can you give the baby a hug and a kiss? = = = children/CHIDES_NA/NewmanRatner/10/5244RE.cha = = = Find an Old Assignment on Accelerate: A Comprehensive Guide Accelerate is a popular learning management system that enables educators and students to manage assignments, coursework, grades, communication, and other academic activities online. If you're looking to find an old assignment on Accelerate, this guide will walk you through the process with clear and concise steps, along with helpful tips and guidelines. Step 1: Log in to your Accelerate account To begin, open your web browser and navigate to the Accelerate login page. Enter your username and password, then click "Log In" to access your dashboard. This is where all your courses are listed, as well as any upcoming or past due assignments. Key tip: Bookmark the Accelerate login page for easy access in the future. Also, make sure to keep your login information secure by avoiding sharing it with anyone else. Step 2: Navigate to the Course Containing the Assignment Once logged in, look at the list of courses displayed on your dashboard. Identify the course containing the old assignment you want to find and click its title to enter the course homepage. Guideline: If you can't remember which course has the assignment, check your email for correspondence from your instructor regarding the specific assignment or review your syllabus if available. Step 3: Access the Assignments Tab After entering the correct course, locate the "Assignments" tab usually positioned near the top menu bar. Clicking on this tab will take you to a new page showing all the assignments created within the course. Key tip: Familiarize yourself with different tabs (e.g., Announcements, Discussions) since they help you quickly access essential resources related to your course. Step 4: Filter and Sort Assignments The Assignments page may contain many entries; therefore, filtering and sorting options might be necessary to narrow down results efficiently. Look out for filters such as 'Due Date,' 'Category,' or 'Type.' Use these filters according to what best describes your old assignment. For instance, select the appropriate due date range or category. Additionally, some platforms allow users to change the display order of assignments using buttons like "Newest," "Oldest," etc. Utilizing these functions makes finding older assignments easier without scrolling extensively. Step 5: Locate the Specific Old Assignment Now that you have filtered and sorted appropriately, browse through the refined list until you spot the target assignment. Remember, titles could include keywords about their content or objective, so pay attention to details while searching. If despite filtering, locating the exact assignment remains challenging, consider employing search functionality provided within the platform. Type relevant words associated with the task into the search box and hit enter. The system should return matching records, including the one(s) sought after. Step 6: Review the Details of the Found Assignment Upon discovering the desired old assignment, click on its title to expand more details about it. These often encompass submission deadlines, attached files, grading criteria, rubrics, and instructional materials. Reviewing these particulars ensures understanding exactly what was expected initially, refreshing memory before attempting revisitation exercises or self-assessment tasks. In conclusion, following these six steps diligently increases chances of successfully finding an old assignment on Accelerate. By mastering navigation techniques and utilizing available features effectively, managing academics digitally becomes less daunting and far more productive. By Cory Duncan Peregrine Falcons are 40 cm. to 50 cm. high. They have a wing span to one Meter. The Peregrine Falcon's feathers are 10 cm. long. When the Peregrine Falcon flies, it looks like a jet flying in the sky. Its beak is yellow and it has brown, black and white feathers. Its features are black around its eyes. The black acts like a shield from the shining sun. The falcon has a lot of pointed feathers and it has a long tail for flying attacks in the air. The Peregrine Falcon needs fast-moving water and tall buildings for a part of its habitat. Some pairs of falcons need one km of space for their territory. They are found in big cities of Canada, including Calgary, Alberta, Montreal, and Edmonton. Peregrine Falcons were becoming endangered because of a pesticide called DDT and other poisons that were sprayed on plants. Actions have taking place to make a law that bands DDT and start a recovery plan . The Peregrine falcon is a fierce hunter. The Peregrine Falcon is also the fastest animal in the world. Sometimes the falcon lives up to 20 years. He eats all sorts of prey. The female adult most of the time grows a little bit bigger than the male. They grow 38 to 50 centimeters. The Peregrine has a pale blue bill and yellow feet. They eat a lot of fish out of the water. When farmers put odd liquids on their buildings, it gets washed into the lakes and rivers by the rain. [Hap reaches into container and gets another figure] Two tower problem This was posted in another thread: Two large towers are erected on a perfectly level piece of ground. We will call them tower A and tower B. Two marks are drawn on the towers near the top. When a line is drawn from the base of tower A to the mark on tower B it is 50 meters long. When a line is drawn from tower B to tower A's mark it is 40 meters long. The two lines intersect between the towers 10 meters above the ground. How far are the towers away from each other? This a fairly thorny problem and there are nice solutions to it over in that thread. Let's see if we can solve it in Geogebra. 1) Put a point at (0,0). It will be called A. 2) Create a slider and set Min at 0 and Max at 40 with an increment of .01. Right click the slider and uncheck Fixed object. 3) Move the slider to 25 and create a point in the input bar of (a,0). It will be called B. Resize your screen until you see both points. 4) Use the circle with center and radius tool and click A and enter 50. 5) Make another one at B with a radius of 40. 6) Draw a perpendicular line to the x axis through B. This will represent the rightmost tower. 7) Use the intersection tool to find the points of intersection of the smaller circle and the y axis. D and C are then created. Hide C and the smaller circle. 8)Use the intersection tool to find the points of intersection of the larger circle and the perpendicular line. F and E are then created. Hide E and larger circle. 9) Use the move graphics tool and stretch out the x axis until the drawing looks well spaced. 10) Draw line segment BD and AF. You will see b =40 and f = 50 in the algebra pane. 11) Get the point of intersection of BD and AF, it will called G. 12) In the input bar enter y(G) to get just the y value of point G. It will be created in the Number column of the algebra pane labeled g. 13) Right click g and drag it to the drawing and place right under the slider so you can see it easily. 14) In options set rounding to 15 decimal places and move the slider until g is as close to 10 as possible. I got g = 10.00700381196065. That is not bad. Can we do better? 15) Right click your slider and set increment to .0001. I got g = 10.000117605127501. 16) Keep reducing the increment until you get as close to 10 as possible. With the smallest increment of .00000001, I got g = 10.000000005695588. Now read the value in the slider, it is a = 37.355085329999866. That is the distance between the towers! It agrees well with the exact answer. You should have a drawing that looks something like the one below. In mathematics, you don't understand things. You just get used to them. Be Miss Alpha: A Comprehensive Guide to Being the Ultimate Female Leader ===================================================================== Introduction: Who is Miss Alpha? ------------------------------- Miss Alpha is a term used to describe a woman who embodies confidence, intelligence, charisma, and strength. She is assertive yet compassionate, ambitious yet grounded, and always strives for personal growth and success. This comprehensive guide will provide you with actionable steps and valuable insights to help you become Miss Alpha, both personally and professionally. Section 1: Building Confidence (Step 1-5) ------------------------------------------ ### Step 1: Embrace Your Strengths and Weaknesses *Explanation*: Understanding your unique qualities allows you to build upon them and address areas where improvement is needed. By acknowledging your weaknesses, you can work towards turning them into strengths or finding ways to compensate for them effectively. *Key Tips & Guidelines*: + Make a list of your top five strengths and weaknesses. + Seek feedback from trusted friends, family members, or colleagues to ensure accuracy. + Develop an action plan to improve upon identified weaknesses while leveraging your strengths. ### Step 2: Practice Self-Care *Explanation*: Taking care of yourself physically, emotionally, and mentally contributes significantly to self-confidence. When you feel good about yourself, others are more likely to perceive you as confident and capable. *Key Tips & Guidelines*: + Establish a consistent exercise routine that works for you. + Maintain a balanced diet rich in nutrients. + Schedule regular downtime and relaxation activities. + Prioritize sleep and maintain a consistent bedtime schedule. ### Step 3: Improve Communication Skills *Explanation*: Effective communication plays a crucial role in building self-assurance and establishing rapport with others. It enables you to express thoughts clearly, listen actively, and negotiate successfully. *Key Tips & Guidelines*: + Work on active listening by focusing on the speaker and summarizing their points. + Use "I" statements instead of blaming language when discussing concerns. Investigate this document (Catalogue ref: LR 2/124/5) This document is part of an inventory from 1651. Whenever a person died it was normal to make a list, or inventory, of all their possessions. This helped the relatives to decide who would inherit particular items. If the dead person owed any debts, the inventory also showed what money and goods there were to pay the debts. - What did this person own when they were alive? Click on the interactive button to explore this document. - What kind of goods are listed here? (Clue: What are Holbein, Leonardo and Michelangelo famous for?) - What can you tell about the person from these goods? To give you an idea of values, 2 in 1651 would be worth around 200 today. - An inventory was a normal document in 1651. Yet this document would have been very shocking to people in 1651. Can you guess why? Did you guess? This is part of the inventory of the goods of King Charles I. The goods listed here were a small part of the king's valuable art collection, which he took great pride in. Why was it shocking in 1651? - In the 1640s nobody in Britain would have even considered that the king would be dead. The execution of the king by his own people in 1649 was an incredible thing to happen. - In those times monarchs were seen as almost like gods. Most people would have been deeply shocked to think that anyone would dare to go through the king's possessions and make a list of them to be sold. This shows how much had changed in Britain by 1651. Then we eat it [pretends to eat]. **Title: How to Divide Polynomials Using Synthetic Division** Synthetic division is a shorthand method used to divide polynomials, particularly when dividing by factors that are linear expressions, such as (x-c), where c is any number. This technique can save time and paper compared to traditional polynomial long division. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to perform synthetic division. ### Step 1: Set Up Your Problem Identify your divisor and dividend. The divisor should be in the form (x-c). For example, if you have the problem (2x^3 - 5x^2 + 7x - 4) divided by (x-1), then 'x-1' is your divisor and the entire trinomial is your dividend. Key Tip: Remember, synthetic division only works when dealing with binomial divisors of the form (x-c). If your divisor does not match this format, consider rewriting it or using regular polynomial long division instead. ### Step 2: Write Down Your Divisor and First Coefficient Write down 'c', which in our case is 1, just outside the box. Then write down the first coefficient of the dividend inside the box. Title: Develop a Culture of Trust: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: A culture of trust is essential for any organization's success as it fosters collaboration, communication, engagement, and innovation among team members. This guide will walk you through the process of developing a strong culture of trust within your workplace or community group by breaking down the steps into actionable items with detailed explanations and helpful tips. Step 1: Define Your Values and Vision * Explanation: Establishing clear values and vision sets the foundation for building a culture of trust. When everyone understands what they are working towards and why, it creates alignment and purpose. * Key Tips: + Involve all stakeholders (employees, managers, volunteers) in creating the values and vision statement. + Make sure the values align with your organization's mission and goals. + Communicate the values and vision frequently so that they become ingrained in day-to-day operations. Step 2: Lead By Example * Explanation: As a leader, demonstrating honesty, transparency, and integrity sends a powerful message about the importance of trust within an organization. * Key Guidelines: + Admit mistakes openly and take responsibility for them. + Share information freely and encourage open dialogue. Title: How to Clean a Bleach Spill on Carpet **Things You'll Need:** * White cloth or paper towels * Distilled white vinegar * Dish soap * Warm water * Spray bottle * Soft-bristled brush (optional) * Towel * Fan (optional) 1. **Blot Up Excess Bleach**: As soon as you notice the bleach spill, quickly grab a clean white cloth or some paper towels. Blot up as much of the excess bleach as possible. Avoid rubbing, as this can push the bleach deeper into the carpet fibers. The goal here is to remove as much liquid as you can before it has a chance to set. 2. **Dilute the Remaining Bleach**: Pour a small amount of distilled white vinegar directly onto the stained area. Vinegar is an acid that neutralizes the alkalinity of bleach, helping to stop any further damage to your carpet fibers. It also acts as a mild cleaning agent. Let the vinegar sit for about 5 minutes. 3. **Create a Cleaning Solution**: While waiting, prepare a cleaning solution by mixing one teaspoon of dish soap with one cup of warm water. Stir until suds form and then pour this into a spray bottle. This gentle yet effective cleaning solution will help lift out the remaining bleach residue without harming your carpet. 4. **Apply the Cleaning Solution**: After five minutes have passed, lightly spray the soapy mixture over the stain using the spray bottle. Be careful not to saturate the carpet; just moisten it enough to make it damp. Too much moisture could lead to mold growth or damage the backing of the carpet. 5. **Gently Scrub the Area**: Using a soft-bristled brush or even a spoon if you don't have a brush, gently scrub the solution into the stain. Work from the outside edge toward the center to prevent spreading the bleach spot. Remember, no harsh rubbing! You want to lift the stain, not grind it in. 6. **Soak Up Liquid**: Once you've worked the cleaning solution into the stain, blot the area again with a fresh dry towel. Continue blotting until no more liquid comes up. If necessary, repeat steps 4 through 6 until the stain disappears. 7. **Rinse With Water**: To ensure all soap is removed, rinse the area with clear warm water. Again, do not soak the carpet. Just dab it with a wet cloth until there are no signs of soap left. 8. **Dry Thoroughly**: Finally, lay down a thick layer of towels over the cleaned area and weigh them down with something heavy like books or weights. Leave these overnight or until the carpet is completely dry. A fan directed at the spot may speed up the drying process. Proper drying prevents mildew formation and color bleeding due to trapped moisture. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** - Always test any cleaning product on an inconspicuous part of your carpet first to check for discoloration or reaction. - Never mix bleach with ammonia or other household cleaners, as toxic gases can be produced. - Act fast when dealing with bleach spills to minimize damage. - For large spills, consider calling a professional cleaner. They have stronger equipment and knowledge to handle extensive damages. Remember, patience and persistence are key when tackling tough stains like those caused by bleach. Good luck! The word "spectrum" is used because not all people with autism have the same traits and difficulties. Some people find interacting more difficult than others. Autism may be very apparent in someone or they may show no outward signs of it. The main areas of difficulty are in social interaction, verbal and nonverbal communication and restricted or repetitive behaviors and interests. In most cases of autism, motor communication {doing things} is affected. All these signs develop in the child's first two to three years of age. Did you put 'em all in there where they went? Nemo needs to go to the doctor's Amelia. Hello young scientists! Today, we are going to learn about something called the "Richardson Constant." You may not have heard of it before, but it's actually really important when it comes to understanding how certain things work in our world. First, let's talk about what thermionic emission is. Thermionic emission happens when heat energy causes electrons to jump off the surface of a material into space. This phenomenon was first observed by Thomas Edison while he was working on his light bulb! He noticed that the filament inside the bulb would start glowing even brighter than usual after being heated up for some time. That's because the hot filament was emitting tiny particles called electrons due to thermionic emission. Now, here comes the Richardson Constant. It is a special number that helps us calculate the rate at which these electrons are emitted from a particular material when it's heated. A higher value of this constant means more electrons will be emitted, leading to a stronger electric current. Imagine trying to fill up balloons with air; having a bigger pump (a higher Richardson Constant) lets you inflate more balloons faster! So why does this matter? Well, think about all those electronic devices around you like smartphones, computers or televisions. They all rely on components called vacuum tubes or cathode ray tubes, which depend heavily on thermionic emission. By knowing the Richardson Constant, engineers can design better electronic devices using materials that emit just the right amount of electrons needed. In summary, the Richardson Constant is crucial in understanding thermionic emission - how heat makes electrons fly off surfaces - so we can build cool gadgets like TVs and phones! Title: How to Detect Pegasus Spyware Pegasus is a powerful spyware developed by the Israeli cyber-arms firm NSO Group. It can infect both iOS and Android devices, giving attackers full access to messages, emails, microphones, cameras, and other sensitive data. This tutorial will guide you through detecting Pegasus spyware on your device. Please note that this process requires advanced technical skills and should only be performed by experienced users or professionals. **Step 1:** Check Unauthorized Data Usage One sign of Pegasus infection is unusual data usage. Monitor your mobile data and Wi-Fi consumption patterns. If there's an unexpected spike in data usage, especially when you haven't changed your habits, it might indicate malicious activity. *Key Tip:* Regularly check your data usage stats in your phone settings. On iOS, go to Settings > Cellular or Settings > Mobile Data; on Android, go to Settings > Network & Internet > Data Usage. **Step 2:** Look for Suspicious Apps Check your installed apps for anything unfamiliar. Cybercriminals often disguise spyware as legitimate applications. *Key Guideline:* Don't just rely on app names; also look at publishers. Be wary of unknown developers or those with poor reviews. **Step 3:** Analyze Device Performance Unusual slowness, frequent crashes, overheating, and rapid battery drain could all point to a compromised system running background processes like Pegasus. *Key Tip:* Use built-in diagnostic tools (like iOS's 'Diagnostics & Usage Data') to identify problematic apps or services. **Step 4:** Examine Outgoing Calls and Messages Review your call logs and message history. Unexplained outbound communications may suggest unauthorized access. *Key Guideline:* Remember that some apps legally send data packets, so don't panic if you see strange entries - investigate thoroughly first. **Step 5:** Perform Network Log Analysis Analyzing network traffic can help detect suspicious activities. Tools such as WireShark allow you to capture and inspect network packets. *Key Warning:* This step requires significant technical knowledge. Improper handling of network analysis tools can cause serious issues. **Step 6:** Run Antivirus Software While antiviruses aren't always effective against sophisticated threats like Pegasus, they can still catch common forms of malware. Running regular scans increases the chances of early detection. *Key Recommendation:* Opt for reputable security software providers who specialize in mobile platforms. **Step 7:** Factory Reset Your Device If you strongly suspect infection but cannot confirm or remove it, consider performing a factory reset. This erases everything, returning your device to its original state. However, ensure you back up important files beforehand! *Key Caution:* A factory reset won't necessarily eliminate persistent threats like Pegasus. Professional assistance is highly recommended. **Step 8:** Seek Professional Help Due to Pegasus' complexity, independent detection isn't guaranteed. Consider reaching out to digital forensics experts or cybersecurity firms specialized in threat hunting and incident response. *Key Advice:* Early intervention prevents further damage. Contact professionals immediately upon suspicion of compromise. Remember, prevention is better than cure. Keep your operating systems updated, avoid jailbreaking/rooting your devices, download apps from trusted sources, regularly update passwords, and educate yourself about online safety measures. Stay vigilant and safe! What else will we see down here. Why do you like the Dark Crystal Ross? = = = children/CHIDES_NA/HSLLD/HV1/ER/admer1.cha = = = I finished [? Finish] playing with it. XXX it's kind of hot I think maybe you want an s... To make a photograph with a camera the "shutter release" button is pressed. Pressing the button opens the "shutter". The shutter is like a door. It covers the hole in the camera box. The shutter is behind the lens. When the shutter is closed no light can enter the camera box. When the shutter is open light can enter the camera. When the button is pressed the shutter opens and then closes. This happens very fast. The amount of time the shutter stays open for is called the "shutter speed". The shutter speed can change between 1/1000th of a second (0.001 s) to a few seconds. Normally the time taken for the shutter to open and close is far less than 1 second. [makes a pile of building blocks in front of Chi.] Adding heat to something increases its temperature, but heat is not the same as temperature. The temperature of an object is the measure of the average speed of the moving particles in it. The energy of the particles is called the internal energy. When an object is heated, its internal energy can increase to make the object hotter. The first law of thermodynamics says that the increase in internal energy is equal to the heat added minus the work done on the surroundings. Read some of the Dr. Seuss early readers, or give a kid a cookie : ) Kids get this. His cookie is bigger, he has more than me. There is no need for workbooks or materials for this. 3) Discrimination of form; recognizes shapes and some letters such as the ones in his name: You may already have a shape sorter or shapes puzzle, but they are not really needed. Cut sandwiches and other foods into squares, rectangles and triangles. Slice apples and oranges across to make circles. Play do you see a circle shape in this room. I love rubber ABC puzzles as they can be used in so many ways, but there are many other tools. Dollar stores often have wooden ABC puzzles or flash cards, especially at back to school time. If you go in with a tight budget dollar stores can save money. Another option is just you and a marker forming letters on a sheet of paper and talking about it. This says Sam, this is the way we write your name. 4) Symbol matching, counting, matching one to one: Again, you can do this with anything. Match blocks, count spoons or cups for dinner. Count how many jumps, how many steps. Pull out old family games and make the rules as simple as possible. Sorry is a great one for recognizing numerals and matching moves. Sometimes there are dominos at the dollar store or in the Target dollar bins; for preschool simply match two ends. 5) Speaking vocabulary: LISTEN! this is the most important thing you can do. Have patience while your child tries to recall words he needs to express himself. 6) Listening vocabulary: Read aloud often. Reread favorite books. Collect authors. Collect illustrators. Collect classics. A book such as Honey for a Child's Heart will help with ideas for what to read. 7) Traces line, cuts with scissors: These fine motor skills are easily learned with construction paper, or whatever is available to you. Make broad lines with markers, first straight then slowly becoming more complex. Your child can trace or cut the lines. Find decent scissors for this that actually cut. Make sure to supervise : ) 8) Skip and hop on one foot: skipping is usually a five-year-old skill so do not get too worried about it. Challenge your children to copy you hopping, jumping, running, walking like a bear or a cat or a duck. 9) Catch and bounce medium size ball: There are many theories about the best kind of ball. You choose! So, this is your shopping list: ABCs puzzle or flashcards, or just some paper. Construction paper, scissors and pencils. Crayons and markers. A ball. You do not need much else. So . The fun part, what do you want? Think about Bible, music, books, art, outdoor play and science. My four-year-old loved the balance scale we gave him for his birthday. My four-year-old grandson loved his big wall map. Paints are fun. Make lists and share them with grandparents and others who like to give gifts. Have fun learning and playing together. He doesn't really do any pretend play yet. Question for Mattie in Guyana: "What is the difference between direct and indirect speech?" Hi Mattie. Thanks for your question. We have quite an extensive explanation about direct and indirect speech in the English4Today online grammar and I've put links through to that in the blog posting and in the grammar FAQ. Let's just have a quick look at it though before you go to the website for more detail and to find out how you form sentences with direct and indirect speech. DIRECT AND REPORTED SPEECH You can answer the question "What did he/she say?" in two ways: - by repeating the words spoken (direct speech) - by reporting the words spoken (indirect or reported speech). Direct speech repeats, or quotes, the exact words spoken. When we use direct speech in writing, we place the words spoken between inverted commas (" .") and there is no change in these words. We may be reporting something that's being said NOW (for example a telephone conversation), or telling someone later about a previous conversation She says "What time will you be home?" She said "What time will you be home?" and I said "I don't know! " "There's a fly in my soup!" screamed Simone. John said, "There's an elephant outside the window." Reported speech is usually used to talk about the past, so we normally change the tense of the words spoken. We use reporting verbs like 'say', 'tell', 'ask', and we may use the word 'that' to introduce the reported words. Inverted commas are not used. She said, "I saw him." She said that she had seen him. - 'That' may be omitted: She told him that she was happy. She told him she was happy. - 'Say' and 'tell': Use 'say' when there is no indirect object: He said that he was tired.Always use 'tell' when you say who was being spoken to (i.e. with an indirect object): He told me that he was tired. 'Talk' and 'speak' are used: - to describe the action of communicating: He talked to us. She was speaking on the telephone. - with 'about' to refer to what was said: He talked (to us) about his parents. LINKS IN THE ENGLISH4TODAY ONLINE GRAMMAR There's a guy who woke up at... Maybe when Mom gets back from the bathroom. Well give me lunch today 'cause I don't like that stuff. Title: How to Change the Default Num Lock State Changing the default Num Lock state can be helpful in various situations. For instance, if you frequently use number keys for typing or gaming, enabling Num Lock by default can save you time. Conversely, disabling Num Lock by default might benefit those who prefer using the traditional arrow keys instead of numeric keypad directions. This tutorial will guide you through changing your system's default Num Lock state on Windows, macOS, and Linux platforms. **Windows:** 1. **Access Device Manager:** Click Start (or press `Win + X`), then select Device Manager from the list that appears. Alternatively, type "Device Manager" into the search bar next to the Start button and click on the app when it shows up in results. ![Device Manager](https://www.wikihow.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Open-Device-Manager-in-Windows-7-Step-2.jpg/aid3650840-v4- Well we'll see how it looks tonight XXX. You would draw an imaginary straight line passing through the 50 centimeters height mark on the vertical axis. Then you would draw a second imaginary line passing through the age of 1 year on the horizontal axis. At the place where the two imaginary lines meet (called their "intersection"), you would then draw a dot. Really, the imaginary lines are usually replaced by "graph paper" (see below). The drawing of this intersection is called ""plotting" the point 50 centimeters for year 1".
